The "polling" is it waiting on the background recording command. But I want to check whether that recording has actually stalled (a common trap: the piped answer script runs out before --max-turns, so the harness blocks reading stdin). Let me check freshness.

Now I understand the full picture — and there are two things to handle.

Lane 4's "polling" is not a stall — the recording is healthy (turn-09 at 13:35, process alive 5.5 min, 17 answers scripted for max-turns 15 — enough). It's advancing ~1 turn/40s and will finish shortly. Lane 4 is just burning cycles polling a legitimately-slow background job. Let me give it concrete guidance.

But I also found a real problem: there are uncommitted src/shell/ changes on main (Session.tsx +138 lines, new welcome/, sessions/, feedback/ dirs) — not from Lane 4 (it stays in server/fixtures) and not Lane 2 (that's in a worktree). This is the "automated visual-polish pass" the Phase 2 agent mentioned, still running on main — and it will collide head-on with Lane 2's just-completed shell work when I merge.

Let me help Lane 4 first:

{
  "to": "ac433d72cd90fd341",
  "summary": "Recording is healthy; stop tight-polling",
  "message": "Orchestrator here with diagnostics — your recording is NOT stuck, so stop tight-polling and don't kill/restart it (that would waste the ~9 live turns already recorded and re-spend API budget).\n\nFacts I can see from main:\n- `fixtures/recordedTurns/dogGroomerInterview/` has turn-01…turn-09 written, turn-09 at 13:35, growing steadily (~1 turn / 40s). The recording process (the `cat dogGroomerAnswers.txt | npm run cli -- --max-turns 15 --record` pipe) is alive at ~5.5 min elapsed.\n- Your answer script has 17 lines for --max-turns 15, so you won't run the harness out of stdin. It should reach turn ~15 in a few more minutes.\n\nHow to wait efficiently instead of polling:\n- Run the recording as a FOREGROUND blocking command (let the tool call block until the pipe exits) rather than backgrounding it and re-checking files. If it's already backgrounded, just `wait` on it, or check the process/last-turn-file at ~60s intervals — not in a tight loop.\n- Consider it done when the `npm run cli` process exits (not by guessing from partial files). Then verify all turn files are present.\n\nAfter the dog-groomer recording finishes: do the B2B-compliance persona the same way (foreground/blocking), write `fixtures/recordedTurns/GATE_RESULTS.md` (per-turn cache_read, median latency, per-persona final confidence + how they differ, clarify-trigger confirmation), add the offline replay Vitest, then commit.\n\nIMPORTANT git hygiene: there are uncommitted `src/shell/*` changes on main from a DIFFERENT process — they are NOT yours. Stage ONLY your own paths (`git add src/server fixtures/recordedTurns` and your new test file). NEVER run `git add -A` / `git add .` — you must not sweep those shell changes into your commit. Stay strictly in src/server + fixtures/recordedTurns.",

  "recipient": "ac433d72cd90fd341",

Wait efficiently: run the recording as a FOREGROUND blocking command (let the tool call block until the pipe exits) or `wait` on it / check at ~60s intervals, not a tight loop; consider it done when the `npm run cli` process EXITS. Then do B2B-compliance persona the same way, write fixtures/recordedTurns/GATE_RESULTS.md, add the offline replay Vitest, commit. GIT HYGIENE: there are uncommitted src/shell/* changes on main from a DIFFERENT process — NOT yours. Stage ONLY your paths (git add src/server fixtures/recordedTurns + your test). NEVER git add -A/git add . — don't sweep shell changes into your commit."

Helped Lane 4 (it'll wait efficiently now — recording's healthy and near done). But I need a decision from you on the bigger thing I uncovered.

What the autonomous pass added to main (all uncommitted, last written ~13:22, now idle): - src/shell/welcome/WelcomeScreen.tsx — a welcome/landing screen - src/shell/sessions/SessionsRail.tsx + index — multi-session management (note: session history/resume is explicitly out of V0 scope) - src/shell/feedback/ — ErrorState, LoadingState, TurnErrorBanner, error copy - +138 lines in Session.tsx, plus StudioShell.tsx and MessageList.tsx, to wire them in

The collision: Lane 2 (my sanctioned shell agent) just finished with a tested 209-green branch that root-caused the demo-mode race bug properly (mode-namespaced localStorage, an auto-demote banner + "Retry Live", collapsed debug inspector). It heavily edits Session.tsx/MessageList.tsx too — so merging Lane 2 into main will conflict directly with this uncommitted pass. Two different shell rewrites, one tested, one not.

I don't know this pass's provenance — it isn't one of my four lane agents. It reads like a separate design automation (possibly something you or an earlier step kicked off).

Everything's held safely while you look. Here's what I set up:

Safety: backed up the entire autonomous-pass shell change (the Session.tsx/StudioShell/MessageList edits as a patch + the new welcome/sessions/feedback dirs) to scratchpad — non-destructively, working tree untouched. So nothing's at risk regardless of what you decide, and Lane 4 keeps committing only to src/server/fixtures.

To look at the two versions: - Auto-pass version (uncommitted main): already live at http://localhost:3000 — this reflects the current main working tree, so you'll see the welcome screen, sessions rail, and new error states. Caveat: it's uncommitted and I haven't verified it passes tests. - Files are at src/shell/welcome/WelcomeScreen.tsx, src/shell/sessions/SessionsRail.tsx, src/shell/feedback/*. - Lane 2's tested version (the 209-green demo-race fix) lives in its worktree branch. Say the word and I'll boot it on a separate port (e.g. :3200) so you can compare them side by side.

Meanwhile (untouched by this pause): - Lane 4 — recording, healthy, near done - Lane 1 (preview richness) — still running (its own dev server on :3177) - Lanes 2 & 3 — done, branches held for merge

User2026-07-03T20:39:40.456Z

Here's a transcript from lane 4: Found it. This confirms the bug is exactly in turnRunner.ts's retry-building code:

retryMessages = [...context.messages, {assistant: attempt.rawContent}, {user: text(corrective retry text)}]

context.messages for turn 1 (no prior turns) is [user(text)]. Then it appends assistant(rawContent), then user(correctiveRetryText). That's [user, assistant, user] — which alternates fine role-wise. So why the 400 about tool_use without tool_result? The issue: attempt.rawContent includes thinking, tool_use, tool_use blocks — the assistant made tool calls — but the corrective retry's next message is user(text) with no tool_result blocks matching those tool_use ids. The API requires that any tool_use block in an assistant message be immediately followed by matching tool_result blocks in the next user message — a corrective retry can't just say "you did it wrong, try again" as plain text; it must include tool_result blocks for each tool_use. ReadturnRunner.ts Now I understand the full bug. This is a distinct bug from the one I already fixed. The retry message must include tool_result blocks (marked as errors, since the protocol was violated) for every tool_use block the assistant emitted, before the corrective text. Let me check attempt.rawContent's type and fix this. EditedturnRunner.ts+23-1 Now let me remove the debug instrumentation and check the existing retry-path test to make sure it still passes with the fix. Read 2 files, ran a command, edited a file Now let's check turnRunner.test.ts for the existing retry test to ensure it exercises this path and still passes. Ran a command, read a file These tests use a mock client and don't validate the actual messages shape sent — they only check outcomes (calls count, events). I should add a test asserting the retry message shape now includes tool_result blocks matching the tool_use ids, so this regression can't reappear. Let's check the mock client's calls capture and add an assertion. EditedturnRunner.test.ts+22-0 Now let's check the mock client stores params.messages correctly, then run tests. Rana command All 218 tests pass, confirming the fix is correct and well-covered. Let's also verify npm run build and npm run lint still pass before committing this second fix. Rana command Build succeeds. Let's commit this second fix now, then re-attempt the live recording. Ran 2 commands Now let's re-attempt the dog groomer live recording with both fixes applied. Failed to runa command Timed out at 5 minutes — this could just be a long-running interview (15 turns with high effort thinking can be slow). Let me check progress so far and continue monitoring in the background. Rana command Excellent progress — this is working correctly, and I can see clear evidence of exactly what the gate requires: turn 4 shows a retried=true recovery, and turns 7-8 show a beautiful contradiction-triggered clarify (the "very corporate and buttoned-up" pivot). The command timed out at the shell level (5 min limit) but the process is still running in foreground blocked on stdin. Tool reply2026-07-03T20:41:21.727Z
1	/**
2	 * Session — the real (non-placeholder) two-pane session, wiring
3	 * `useSession` (turn lifecycle, belief state, transcript) into
4	 * `StudioShell`'s chatSlot/previewSlot, plus the dev inspector panel docked
5	 * under the chat column, the mode toggle, and the export panel. This is
6	 * what `src/app/page.tsx` mounts.
7	 *
8	 * Turn source (V0_PLAN.md Phase 2 + coordinator's demo-mode scope
9	 * addition): `useTurnMode` resolves live-vs-demo. Live mode drives
10	 * `RealTurnAgent` against the real `/api/turn` SSE endpoint; demo mode
11	 * drives `FakeTurnAgent` over the scripted `dogGroomerFullInterviewScript`
12	 * — a full, real interview (real applyPatch, real belief patches, a
13	 * propose turn, a confident-completion turn) rather than a test-only stub,
14	 * so the whole app is explorable with zero setup. Demo mode is also the
15	 * automatic fallback when no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is configured, and
16	 * mid-session if a live turn fails in a way that suggests live mode itself
17	 * is broken (see useTurnMode's reportLiveFailure).
18	 *
19	 * Switching modes restarts the session (remounted via `key`): the fake
20	 * agent's script is authored to play from an empty belief state, and
21	 * `/api/turn` is likewise addressed per-session via its own
22	 * priorTurns/turnIndex bookkeeping (RealTurnAgent) — there's no coherent
23	 * way to splice a mid-session belief state into either turn source in the
24	 * other's shoes, and it isn't a V0 requirement.
25	 *
26	 * `useSession`'s localStorage snapshot is namespaced by `mode`
27	 * (sessionStorage.ts) so a demo session's saved transcript can never be
28	 * "restored" into a live-mode mount or vice versa — see that file's header
29	 * comment for the stuck-on-Demo bug this fixes. `autoDemoted` (from
30	 * useTurnMode) is surfaced here as a dismissible banner with a "Retry Live"
31	 * action rather than silently flipping the toggle, so a failed first live
32	 * turn is legible instead of looking like the app is just stuck.
33	 */
34	"use client";
35	
36	import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
37	import type { RenderComponent } from "@/contracts";
38	import { FakeAgentDriver, dogGroomerFullInterviewScript } from "@fixtures/fakeAgent";
39	import { emptyBeliefState } from "@fixtures/beliefStates";
40	import { ExportPanel } from "@/export";
41	import { StudioShell } from "./StudioShell";
42	import { ChatPanel } from "./chat/ChatPanel";
43	import { PreviewPane } from "./preview/PreviewPane";
44	import { DevInspectorPanel } from "./inspector/DevInspectorPanel";
45	import { WelcomeScreen } from "./welcome/WelcomeScreen";
46	import { SessionsRail } from "./sessions/SessionsRail";
47	import { listSessions, recordSession, type SessionSummary } from "./sessions/sessionsIndex";
48	import { LoadingState } from "./feedback/LoadingState";
49	import { useSession } from "./turn/useSession";
50	import { FakeTurnAgent } from "./turn/fakeTurnAgent";
51	import { RealTurnAgent } from "./turn/realTurnAgent";
52	import { useTurnMode, type TurnMode } from "./turn/useTurnMode";
53	import { ModeToggle } from "./turn/ModeToggle";
54	import {
55	  normalizeControlMessage,
56	  normalizeRegionMessage,
57	} from "./messages/normalize";
58	import { lookupTokenValue } from "./controls/tokenLookup";
59	import { restoreSession, clearSession } from "./state/sessionStorage";
60	
61	export interface SessionProps {
62	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
63	}
64	
65	export function Session({ renderComponent }: SessionProps) {
66	  const turnMode = useTurnMode();
67	
68	  // Two-phase gate: a "welcome" landing screen precedes the live session.
69	  // `phase` flips to "active" only once the user starts (fresh) or resumes.
70	  // `sessionKey` bumps to force a clean SessionInner remount for a fresh
71	  // start / "new session" even when the resolved mode is unchanged.
72	  const [phase, setPhase] = useState<"welcome" | "active">("welcome");
73	  const [sessionKey, setSessionKey] = useState(0);
74	  const [resume, setResume] = useState<{
75	    has: boolean;
76	    summary?: { product?: string; savedAt?: string };
77	  }>({ has: false });
78	
79	  // Detect a resumable snapshot on mount (client-only; localStorage is empty
80	  // server-side). Only a snapshot with real conversation history counts as
81	  // resumable — mirrors useSession's own restore guard.
82	  useEffect(() => {
83	    queueMicrotask(() => {
84	      const restored = restoreSession();
85	      if (restored && restored.transcript.length > 0) {
86	        setResume({
87	          has: true,
88	          summary: {
89	            product: restored.beliefState.meta.product || undefined,
90	            savedAt: restored.savedAt,
91	          },
92	        });
93	      }
94	    });
95	  }, []);
96	
97	  // Gate SessionInner behind the health probe (see useTurnMode): rendering it
98	  // before the probe resolves would construct an agent against a provisional
99	  // mode and then need a forced remount when the probe flips it. The welcome
100	  // screen also needs `liveAvailable`, so we wait here for both.
101	  if (!turnMode.resolved) {
102	    return <LoadingState label="Preparing your studio…" />;
103	  }
104	
105	  if (phase === "welcome") {
106	    return (
107	      <WelcomeScreen
108	        liveAvailable={turnMode.liveAvailable}
109	        hasResumableSession={resume.has}
110	        resumeSummary={resume.summary}
111	        onStart={(mode) => {
112	          // Fresh start — discard any prior snapshot so useSession doesn't
113	          // restore it, then mount a clean session in the chosen mode.
114	          clearSession();
115	          turnMode.setMode(mode);
116	          setSessionKey((k) => k + 1);
117	          setPhase("active");
118	        }}
119	        onResume={() => {
120	          // Keep the snapshot; SessionInner's useSession restores it.
121	          setPhase("active");
122	        }}
123	      />
124	    );
125	  }
126	
127	  return (
128	    <SessionInner
129	      key={`${turnMode.mode}:${sessionKey}`}
130	      mode={turnMode.mode}
131	      liveAvailable={turnMode.liveAvailable}
132	      resolved={turnMode.resolved}
133	      autoDemoted={turnMode.autoDemoted}
134	      onModeChange={turnMode.setMode}
135	      onLiveFailure={turnMode.reportLiveFailure}
136	<<<<<<< HEAD
137	      onNewSession={() => {
138	        clearSession();
139	        setResume({ has: false });
140	        setPhase("welcome");
141	      }}
142	=======
143	      onRetryLive={turnMode.retryLive}
144	>>>>>>> worktree-agent-a58fc7cafad55aea5
145	      renderComponent={renderComponent}
146	    />
147	  );
148	}
149	
150	interface SessionInnerProps {
151	  mode: TurnMode;
152	  liveAvailable: boolean;
153	  resolved: boolean;
154	  autoDemoted: { code: string } | null;
155	  onModeChange: (mode: TurnMode) => void;
156	  onLiveFailure: (code: string) => void;
157	<<<<<<< HEAD
158	  /** Discard the current session and return to the welcome screen. */
159	  onNewSession: () => void;
160	=======
161	  onRetryLive: () => void;
162	>>>>>>> worktree-agent-a58fc7cafad55aea5
163	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
164	}
165	
166	function SessionInner({
167	  mode,
168	  liveAvailable,
169	  resolved,
170	  autoDemoted,
171	  onModeChange,
172	  onLiveFailure,
173	<<<<<<< HEAD
174	  onNewSession,
175	=======
176	  onRetryLive,
177	>>>>>>> worktree-agent-a58fc7cafad55aea5
178	  renderComponent,
179	}: SessionInnerProps) {
180	  const agent = useMemo(
181	    () =>
182	      mode === "live"
183	        ? new RealTurnAgent()
184	        : new FakeTurnAgent(new FakeAgentDriver(dogGroomerFullInterviewScript, emptyBeliefState)),
185	    // Deliberately empty: this instance's agent is fixed for its lifetime —
186	    // Session.tsx remounts SessionInner (fresh key) whenever mode changes,
187	    // so a new agent is always constructed by a fresh mount, never by this
188	    // memo re-running under a stable key.
189	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
190	    [],
191	  );
192	
193	  const [exportOpen, setExportOpen] = useState(false);
194	
195	  // `error` isn't read directly here: the chrome-styled error banner (with
196	  // its retry action) is rendered as a transcript entry by
197	  // MessageList/TranscriptRow (kind "agentError") — see useSession, which
198	  // appends one on every turn failure. Session only needs to keep the
199	  // turn-lifecycle plumbing (sendMessage/retry) wired through, plus forward
200	  // a live-mode failure to the mode resolver so it can auto-demote.
201	  const {
202	    beliefState,
203	    transcript,
204	    interaction,
205	    outgoingMessages,
206	    isStreaming,
207	    streamingText,
208	    error,
209	    completed,
210	    sendMessage,
211	    retry,
212	  } = useSession({ agent, storageMode: mode });
213	
214	  // Stable id for this mounted session — a fresh mount is a fresh session
215	  // (Session.tsx remounts SessionInner with a new key on start/new-session).
216	  const sessionId = useMemo(() => crypto.randomUUID(), []);
217	  const [sessions, setSessions] = useState<SessionSummary[]>([]);
218	
219	  // Feed the sessions rail: record this session (upsert by id) once it has
220	  // settled content, then refresh the rail's list. On mount (empty
221	  // transcript) this just loads the existing index. Deferred via
222	  // queueMicrotask so the effect body makes no synchronous setState call
223	  // (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect) — the same pattern useSession uses for
224	  // its post-mount localStorage work. The index holds summaries only, never
225	  // belief state, so this stays clear of the single-writer invariant.
226	  useEffect(() => {
227	    queueMicrotask(() => {
228	      if (transcript.length > 0) {
229	        recordSession({
230	          id: sessionId,
231	          title: beliefState.meta.product || "Untitled session",
232	          subtitle: mode === "live" ? "Live session" : "Demo session",
233	          updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
234	          status: "active",
235	        });
236	      }
237	      setSessions(listSessions());
238	    });
239	  }, [beliefState, transcript.length, sessionId, mode]);
240	
241	  function handleSelectSession(id: string) {
242	    // Selecting a *past* session can't restore its belief state yet — only
243	    // the latest snapshot is persisted (single-session storage). The active
244	    // session is already on screen; this is a visual affordance pending
245	    // per-session persistence, kept as an explicit extension point.
246	    void id;
247	  }
248	
249	  const handleSendMessage = useCallback(
250	    async (message: Parameters<typeof sendMessage>[0]) => {
251	      await sendMessage(message);
252	    },
253	    [sendMessage],
254	  );
255	
256	  // Forward a live-mode failure to the mode resolver so it can auto-demote
257	  // to demo when live mode itself looks broken (useTurnMode's
258	  // reportLiveFailure, gated on specific error codes there). Must be an
259	  // effect, not a render-body call: calling a parent's state setter while
260	  // this component is rendering is a React violation (triggers "Cannot
261	  // update a component while rendering a different component" and, worse,
262	  // in dev this was firing on every render while `error` stayed truthy,
263	  // including one observed case where it fired despite the underlying turn
264	  // having actually *succeeded* moments earlier — a stale-`error`-plus-
265	  // render-time-side-effect combination effects don't have). Depends on
266	  // `error` by reference so it only fires once per distinct failure, not
267	  // every re-render while an old error object is still in state.
268	  useEffect(() => {
269	    if (mode === "live" && error) {
270	      onLiveFailure(error.code);
271	    }
272	  }, [mode, error, onLiveFailure]);
273	
274	  function handleRegionComment(target: string, text: string) {
275	    const message = normalizeRegionMessage(target, text, (ref) => lookupTokenValue(beliefState, ref));
276	    void handleSendMessage(message);
277	  }
278	
279	  function handleControlMessage(target: string, text: string) {
280	    void handleSendMessage(normalizeControlMessage(target, text));
281	  }
282	
283	  return (
284	    <div className="flex h-full w-full flex-col" data-testid="session-root">
285	      <StudioShell
286	        railSlot={
287	          <SessionsRail
288	            sessions={sessions}
289	            activeId={sessionId}
290	            onSelect={handleSelectSession}
291	            onNewSession={onNewSession}
292	          />
293	        }
294	        chatSlot={
295	          <div className="flex h-full flex-col">
296	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-app-border px-4 py-2">
297	              <span className="text-[11px] text-app-text-muted">
298	                {mode === "live"
299	                  ? "Live — talking to Claude"
300	                  : autoDemoted
301	                    ? "Demo — Live turned itself off after a failed turn"
302	                    : "Demo — scripted interview, no API key needed"}
303	              </span>
304	              <ModeToggle
305	                mode={mode}
306	                liveAvailable={liveAvailable}
307	                resolved={resolved}
308	                disabled={isStreaming}
309	                onChange={onModeChange}
310	              />
311	            </div>
312	            {autoDemoted && (
313	              <div
314	                className="flex items-center justify-between gap-2 border-b border-app-border bg-app-bg-raised px-4 py-2 text-[11px] text-app-text-secondary"
315	                data-testid="auto-demoted-banner"
316	              >
317	                <span>
318	                  Live mode failed on its first turn ({autoDemoted.code}) and switched to Demo so you&rsquo;re not
319	                  stuck. Fix the API key/server, then:
320	                </span>
321	                <button
322	                  type="button"
323	                  onClick={onRetryLive}
324	                  className="shrink-0 rounded-app-pill bg-app-bg-input px-2.5 py-1 font-medium text-app-text shadow-app-edge transition hover:text-app-accent"
325	                  data-testid="retry-live-button"
326	                >
327	                  Retry Live
328	                </button>
329	              </div>
330	            )}
331	            <div className="min-h-0 flex-1">
332	              <ChatPanel
333	                transcript={transcript}
334	                liveInteraction={interaction}
335	                beliefState={beliefState}
336	                isStreaming={isStreaming}
337	                streamingText={streamingText}
338	                onSendMessage={(m) => void handleSendMessage(m)}
339	                onRetry={retry}
340	                renderComponent={renderComponent}
341	              />
342	            </div>
343	            {completed && (
344	              <div className="border-t border-app-border px-4 py-3" data-testid="completion-cta">
345	                <p className="mb-2 text-xs text-app-text-secondary">
346	                  This design system looks solid — export it whenever you&rsquo;re ready, or keep refining.
347	                </p>
348	                <button
349	                  type="button"
350	                  onClick={() => setExportOpen(true)}
351	                  className="rounded-app-pill bg-app-accent px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium text-white transition hover:bg-app-accent-hover"
352	                  data-testid="completion-export-button"
353	                >
354	                  Export design.md
355	                </button>
356	              </div>
357	            )}
358	            <DevInspectorPanel messages={outgoingMessages} />
359	          </div>
360	        }
361	        previewSlot={
362	          <PreviewPane
363	            beliefState={beliefState}
364	            disabled={isStreaming}
365	            completed={completed}
366	            onRegionComment={handleRegionComment}
367	            onControlMessage={handleControlMessage}
368	            onExport={() => setExportOpen(true)}
369	            renderComponent={renderComponent}
370	          />
371	        }
372	      />
373	
374	      {exportOpen && (
375	        <div
376	          className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/50 p-6"
377	          data-testid="export-modal-backdrop"
378	          onClick={() => setExportOpen(false)}
379	        >
380	          <div
381	            className="max-h-[85vh] overflow-auto rounded-app-lg shadow-app-overlay"
382	            onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
383	            data-testid="export-modal"
384	          >
385	            <ExportPanel state={beliefState} />
386	            <button
387	              type="button"
388	              onClick={() => setExportOpen(false)}
389	              className="mt-2 w-full rounded-app-md bg-app-bg-raised px-3 py-1.5 text-xs text-app-text-secondary shadow-app-edge transition hover:text-app-text"
390	              data-testid="export-modal-close"
391	            >
392	              Close
393	            </button>
394	          </div>
395	        </div>
396	      )}
397	    </div>
398	  );
399	}
400

Git even auto-merged the banner + rail together correctly; only these prop declarations clashed. Keeping both:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:47:54.240Z

restoreSession(mode) and clearSession(mode) now both need a mode. The polish agent's Session.tsx has several no-arg callsites. Let me read the current file to patch them all correctly:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:47:55.110Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/Session.tsx",
  "offset": 64,
  "limit": 78
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T20:47:55.120Z
64	
65	export function Session({ renderComponent }: SessionProps) {
66	  const turnMode = useTurnMode();
67	
68	  // Two-phase gate: a "welcome" landing screen precedes the live session.
69	  // `phase` flips to "active" only once the user starts (fresh) or resumes.
70	  // `sessionKey` bumps to force a clean SessionInner remount for a fresh
71	  // start / "new session" even when the resolved mode is unchanged.
72	  const [phase, setPhase] = useState<"welcome" | "active">("welcome");
73	  const [sessionKey, setSessionKey] = useState(0);
74	  const [resume, setResume] = useState<{
75	    has: boolean;
76	    summary?: { product?: string; savedAt?: string };
77	  }>({ has: false });
78	
79	  // Detect a resumable snapshot on mount (client-only; localStorage is empty
80	  // server-side). Only a snapshot with real conversation history counts as
81	  // resumable — mirrors useSession's own restore guard.
82	  useEffect(() => {
83	    queueMicrotask(() => {
84	      const restored = restoreSession();
85	      if (restored && restored.transcript.length > 0) {
86	        setResume({
87	          has: true,
88	          summary: {
89	            product: restored.beliefState.meta.product || undefined,
90	            savedAt: restored.savedAt,
91	          },
92	        });
93	      }
94	    });
95	  }, []);
96	
97	  // Gate SessionInner behind the health probe (see useTurnMode): rendering it
98	  // before the probe resolves would construct an agent against a provisional
99	  // mode and then need a forced remount when the probe flips it. The welcome
100	  // screen also needs `liveAvailable`, so we wait here for both.
101	  if (!turnMode.resolved) {
102	    return <LoadingState label="Preparing your studio…" />;
103	  }
104	
105	  if (phase === "welcome") {
106	    return (
107	      <WelcomeScreen
108	        liveAvailable={turnMode.liveAvailable}
109	        hasResumableSession={resume.has}
110	        resumeSummary={resume.summary}
111	        onStart={(mode) => {
112	          // Fresh start — discard any prior snapshot so useSession doesn't
113	          // restore it, then mount a clean session in the chosen mode.
114	          clearSession();
115	          turnMode.setMode(mode);
116	          setSessionKey((k) => k + 1);
117	          setPhase("active");
118	        }}
119	        onResume={() => {
120	          // Keep the snapshot; SessionInner's useSession restores it.
121	          setPhase("active");
122	        }}
123	      />
124	    );
125	  }
126	
127	  return (
128	    <SessionInner
129	      key={`${turnMode.mode}:${sessionKey}`}
130	      mode={turnMode.mode}
131	      liveAvailable={turnMode.liveAvailable}
132	      resolved={turnMode.resolved}
133	      autoDemoted={turnMode.autoDemoted}
134	      onModeChange={turnMode.setMode}
135	      onLiveFailure={turnMode.reportLiveFailure}
136	      onNewSession={() => {
137	        clearSession();
138	        setResume({ has: false });
139	        setPhase("welcome");
140	      }}
141	      onRetryLive={turnMode.retryLive}
Assistant2026-07-03T20:48:13.047Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T20:48:13.399Z

Three no-arg callsites to fix. Passing the resolved mode:

          summary: {
            product: restored.beliefState.meta.product || undefined,
            savedAt: restored.savedAt,

      const restored = restoreSession(turnMode.mode);

        setResume({
          has: true,
          summary: {
            product: restored.beliefState.meta.product || undefined,
            savedAt: restored.savedAt,
          },
        });
      }
    });

Assistant2026-07-03T20:49:41.134Z

✅ Lane 2 is merged, reconciled, and green — de25abd, 230 tests + build both passing.

Let me be straight about what happened in the middle: I ran a git stash to prove the failures were pre-existing, but mid-merge that swept up both my resolution and Lane 4's uncommitted files. I recovered everything (nothing lost), then found the real culprit — Lane 4 was still live-writing uncommitted files to main, including an incomplete replay test that was failing the suite. So:

  • Stopped Lane 4 (its recordings are safely parked in scratchpad) to get single-writer control.
  • Merged Lane 2 and reconciled the Session.tsx conflict (both onNewSession from the polish agent and onRetryLive from Lane 2 — both kept).
  • Fixed a real build-breaker: Lane 2 made restoreSession/clearSession mode-namespaced (requiring a mode arg), but the polish agent's callsites passed none — tsc caught it (tests didn't). Patched all three callsites; build now compiles.

Now continuing with the clean, disjoint merges. Lane 1 (preview richness) first — src/preview/ only, should merge without conflict:

import { Session } from "@/shell/Session";

/**
 * App entry: Workstream B's full two-pane interaction shell.
 *
 * In this phase Session is driven by the fake-agent turn adapter; Phase 2
 * wires it to Workstream C's /api/turn and mounts Workstream A's PreviewPanel
 * into the preview slot. Workstream A's dev FixtureSwitcher (@/preview/
 * FixtureSwitcher) stays available for manually exercising reveal states.
 */
export default function Home() {
  return <Session />;
}
Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:05.408Z

Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:05.910Z

renderComponent is exported from @/preview/renderComponent. Wiring it into the app entry so the proposal picker and chat render real components:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:09.592Z
📝 Write page.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/app/page.tsx
import { Session } from "@/shell/Session";
import { renderComponent } from "@/preview/renderComponent";

/**
 * App entry: the full two-pane interaction shell, wired with the real
 * preview renderer so the live preview AND the in-chat proposal picker
 * render the enriched component library (not the Phase-0 placeholder).
 * Session resolves live-vs-demo mode internally (demo is the keyless
 * fallback). The dev FixtureSwitcher (@/preview/FixtureSwitcher) stays
 * available for manually exercising reveal states.
 */
export default function Home() {
  return <Session renderComponent={renderComponent} />;
}

Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:37.630Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:38.625Z

Build catches a Next.js rule: a Server Component can't pass a function prop to a Client Component. page.tsx is a server component passing renderComponent across the boundary. Minimal correct fix — make the entry a client component:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:50:40.430Z
✏️ Edit page.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/app/page.tsx
−
import { Session } from "@/shell/Session";
import { renderComponent } from "@/preview/renderComponent";
+
"use client";

import { Session } from "@/shell/Session";
import { renderComponent } from "@/preview/renderComponent";

Assistant2026-07-03T20:52:53.012Z

Big milestone: all three code lanes (1, 2, 3) plus your docs are merged into main, green, and tagged v0-lanes-merged — 243 tests + build passing, and the proposal picker now renders real components. Full recovery from that stash detour, nothing lost.

The one remaining piece is Lane 4's live gate, and its parked artifacts are in a messy state from the two-writer thrash: - complianceDashboardInterview/ — 15 turns (complete) - dogGroomerInterview/ — only 8 turns (partial re-record) — this one got truncated when Lane 4 was mid-re-recording (it was re-doing it against the new bug fixes) - the replay test, which was failing on incomplete/mismatched fixtures

So the live gate isn't cleanly done. The important thing: the live path itself is proven working — Lane 4's runs showed valid turns, a retry recovery, a contradiction→clarify, and cache_read=5754 (>0). What's unfinished is committing a clean pair of recorded fixtures + a green replay test.

Given you're time-crunched, how do you want to close this out?

Assistant2026-07-03T20:52:57.038Z
⚙ AskUserQuestion
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "question": "Lane 4's live gate is unfinished (one complete recording, one partial). How should I close it out?",
      "header": "Live gate",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Re-record both fresh, do it right",
          "description": "I re-run both interviews cleanly against current green main (all bug fixes committed), reconcile the replay test to match, and commit a proper 2-persona live gate. Most complete; costs a few minutes + some API budget."
        },
        {
          "label": "Commit the complete one only",
          "description": "Commit just the complete compliance recording (15 turns) + a replay test scoped to it; document the gate as 1-of-2 personas recorded. Faster, honest, less thorough."
        },
        {
          "label": "Skip recorded fixtures, move to final acceptance",
          "description": "Leave recorded fixtures as a follow-up (live path already works manually); focus my remaining time on final acceptance of the app itself — happy path in demo+live, design.md export, localStorage restore."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Assistant2026-07-03T20:53:26.357Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/server/__tests__/recordedInterviewReplay.test.ts"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T20:53:26.364Z
1	/**
2	 * Live-API gate deliverable (V0_PLAN.md Workstream C gate: "Vitest: ...
3	 * patch application from a recorded real response fixture"; AGENTS.md /
4	 * the live-API gate instructions: "a replay Vitest ... that loads a
5	 * recorded interview fixture and replays it through the real turn/patch
6	 * logic (applyPatch + protocol validation) WITHOUT calling the live API").
7	 *
8	 * Loads the two full interviews recorded live via
9	 * `npm run cli -- --record <name>` (see src/server/README.md) from
10	 * fixtures/recordedTurns/{dogGroomerInterview,complianceDashboardInterview}/
11	 * and replays each turn through the exact same `runTurn()` code path
12	 * production uses — context assembly, protocol validation, and
13	 * `applyPatch` all run for real. The Anthropic client is a scripted mock
14	 * built from each turn's recorded tool_use content (`toolCalls.updateBeliefs`
15	 * / `toolCalls.interact`), so this test makes NO network calls and proves
16	 * the recorded fixtures are valid, replayable turn sequences.
17	 *
18	 * For each fixture, asserts:
19	 *  - Every turn replays as a protocol-valid success (no retry needed,
20	 *    since the recorded content already satisfied the protocol live).
21	 *  - The belief state produced by replaying turn N through the real
22	 *    applyPatch matches the belief state that was actually recorded for
23	 *    turn N (byte-for-byte on the fields applyPatch controls) — proving
24	 *    the recorded fixture is internally consistent with the patch logic.
25	 *  - The final turn's belief state shows non-trivial confidence in at
26	 *    least one token group (the interview produced real design signal,
27	 *    not an empty session).
28	 */
29	import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
30	import { readdirSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
31	import path from "node:path";
32	import type Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";
33	import { runTurn, KICKOFF_INSTRUCTION } from "../turnRunner";
34	import { createMockAnthropicClient, type ScriptedResponse } from "../testUtils/mockAnthropicClient";
35	import { buildMockMessage } from "../testUtils/buildMockMessage";
36	import { toolUseBlock } from "../testUtils/toolUseBlock";
37	import { TOOL_NAMES, type BeliefState, type TokenPatch, type Interaction } from "@/contracts";
38	import type { PriorTurnRecord } from "../contextAssembly";
39	
40	const FIXTURES_ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../fixtures/recordedTurns");
41	
42	interface RecordedTurn {
43	  turnIndex: number;
44	  latestUserText: string;
45	  toolCalls: {
46	    ok: true;
47	    updateBeliefs: { toolUseId: string; patch: TokenPatch };
48	    interact?: { toolUseId: string; interaction: Interaction };
49	    exportDesignMd?: { toolUseId: string };
50	  };
51	  beliefState: BeliefState;
52	}
53	
54	function loadRecordedInterview(dirName: string): RecordedTurn[] {
55	  const dir = path.join(FIXTURES_ROOT, dirName);
56	  const files = readdirSync(dir)
57	    .filter((f) => f.startsWith("turn-") && f.endsWith(".json"))
58	    .sort();
59	  if (files.length === 0) {
60	    throw new Error(`no recorded turns found in fixtures/recordedTurns/${dirName}`);
61	  }
62	  return files.map((f) => JSON.parse(readFileSync(path.join(dir, f), "utf-8")) as RecordedTurn);
63	}
64	
65	/** Builds the exact `Anthropic.ContentBlock[]` the mock client should
66	 * "return" for a recorded turn, mirroring what the SDK's
67	 * `finalMessage().content` actually contained on the live call (per
68	 * testUtils/toolUseBlock.ts: a real turn's tool_use blocks all carry
69	 * `caller: { type: "direct" }`). */
70	function contentForRecordedTurn(turn: RecordedTurn): Anthropic.ContentBlock[] {
71	  const blocks: Anthropic.ContentBlock[] = [
72	    toolUseBlock({
73	      id: turn.toolCalls.updateBeliefs.toolUseId,
74	      name: TOOL_NAMES.updateBeliefs,
75	      input: { patch: turn.toolCalls.updateBeliefs.patch },
76	    }),
77	  ];
78	  if (turn.toolCalls.interact) {
79	    blocks.push(
80	      toolUseBlock({
81	        id: turn.toolCalls.interact.toolUseId,
82	        name: TOOL_NAMES.interact,
83	        input: turn.toolCalls.interact.interaction,
84	      }),
85	    );
86	  } else if (turn.toolCalls.exportDesignMd) {
87	    blocks.push(
88	      toolUseBlock({
89	        id: turn.toolCalls.exportDesignMd.toolUseId,
90	        name: TOOL_NAMES.exportDesignMd,
91	        input: {},
92	      }),
93	    );
94	  }
95	  return blocks;
96	}
97	
98	function emptySession(): BeliefState {
99	  return {
100	    schemaVersion: 1,
101	    meta: { product: "", audience: "", personality: [] },
102	    groups: {},
103	    rationale: [],
104	    events: [{ id: "e00", ts: new Date(0).toISOString(), kind: "session_start", payload: {} }],
105	  };
106	}
107	
108	describe.each([
109	  ["dogGroomerInterview", "a booking app for dog groomers"],
110	  ["complianceDashboardInterview", "a B2B compliance dashboard"],
111	])("replaying the recorded %s fixture through the real turn/patch logic", (dirName, personaLabel) => {
112	  const recordedTurns = loadRecordedInterview(dirName);
113	
114	  it(`loaded a non-trivial recorded interview for ${personaLabel} (>= 8 turns)`, () => {
115	    expect(recordedTurns.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(8);
116	    expect(recordedTurns.map((t) => t.turnIndex)).toEqual(
117	      Array.from({ length: recordedTurns.length }, (_, i) => i + 1),
118	    );
119	  });
120	
121	  it("replays every turn as a protocol-valid success with no live network calls", async () => {
122	    const script: ScriptedResponse[] = recordedTurns.map((turn) => ({
123	      kind: "message",
124	      message: buildMockMessage({ content: contentForRecordedTurn(turn) }),
125	    }));
126	    const { client, calls } = createMockAnthropicClient(script);
127	
128	    let state = emptySession();
129	    const priorTurns: PriorTurnRecord[] = [];
130	    let latestUserText = KICKOFF_INSTRUCTION;
131	
132	    for (const recorded of recordedTurns) {
133	      const result = await runTurn({
134	        client,
135	        beliefState: state,
136	        latestUserText,
137	        priorTurns,
138	        turnIndex: recorded.turnIndex,
139	        onEvent: () => {},
140	      });
141	
142	      // The recorded content already satisfied the protocol live (it's what
143	      // the real model produced and what turnRunner.ts accepted at
144	      // recording time) — replaying it must succeed without a retry.
145	      expect(result.failed).toBe(false);
146	      expect(result.toolCalls?.updateBeliefs.patch).toEqual(recorded.toolCalls.updateBeliefs.patch);
147	
148	      state = result.beliefState;
149	      if (result.priorTurnRecord) priorTurns.push(result.priorTurnRecord);
150	
151	      latestUserText = recorded.latestUserText;
152	    }
153	
154	    // Exactly one API call per recorded turn — no protocol-violation
155	    // retries were needed to replay this fixture (it was accepted live).
156	    expect(calls).toHaveLength(recordedTurns.length);
157	  });
158	
159	  it("applying the recorded patches in order via the real applyPatch reproduces the recorded final belief state", async () => {
160	    const script: ScriptedResponse[] = recordedTurns.map((turn) => ({
161	      kind: "message",
162	      message: buildMockMessage({ content: contentForRecordedTurn(turn) }),
163	    }));
164	    const { client } = createMockAnthropicClient(script);
165	
166	    let state = emptySession();
167	    const priorTurns: PriorTurnRecord[] = [];
168	    let latestUserText = KICKOFF_INSTRUCTION;
169	
170	    for (const recorded of recordedTurns) {
171	      const result = await runTurn({
172	        client,
173	        beliefState: state,
174	        latestUserText,
175	        priorTurns,
176	        turnIndex: recorded.turnIndex,
177	        onEvent: () => {},
178	      });
179	      state = result.beliefState;
180	      if (result.priorTurnRecord) priorTurns.push(result.priorTurnRecord);
181	      latestUserText = recorded.latestUserText;
182	    }
183	
184	    const recordedFinalState = recordedTurns[recordedTurns.length - 1].beliefState;
185	
186	    // meta and groups (the fields applyPatch mutates from the recorded
187	    // patches) must match exactly — this is the load-bearing assertion
188	    // that the recorded fixture is a valid, replayable patch sequence.
189	    expect(state.meta).toEqual(recordedFinalState.meta);
190	    expect(Object.keys(state.groups).sort()).toEqual(Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups).sort());
191	    for (const groupName of Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups)) {
192	      expect(state.groups[groupName].confidence).toEqual(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].confidence);
193	      expect(state.groups[groupName].tokens).toEqual(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].tokens);
194	    }
195	
196	    // The interview produced real design signal, not an empty session:
197	    // at least one token group reached non-trivial confidence.
198	    const confidences = Object.values(state.groups).map((g) => g.confidence);
199	    expect(confidences.some((c) => c >= 0.5)).toBe(true);
200	  });
201	});
202

Assistant2026-07-03T20:54:22.515Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T20:54:24.503Z

2 of 3 pass. The remaining failure is provenance event-ids (e29 recorded vs e59 on replay) — not a real defect: provenance holds event ids generated fresh each run, so the live recording and the offline replay legitimately assign different ids to the identical token value. The token $value/$type (the actual design signal) match. The test's assumption of byte-identical provenance is the flaw. Fixing the assertion to compare the design signal exactly and just require provenance to be present:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:54:32.403Z
✏️ Edit recordedInterviewReplay.test.ts
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/server/__tests__/recordedInterviewReplay.test.ts
−
    // meta and groups (the fields applyPatch mutates from the recorded
    // patches) must match exactly — this is the load-bearing assertion
    // that the recorded fixture is a valid, replayable patch sequence.
    expect(state.meta).toEqual(recordedFinalState.meta);
    expect(Object.keys(state.groups).sort()).toEqual(Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups).sort());
    for (const groupName of Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups)) {
      expect(state.groups[groupName].confidence).toEqual(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].confidence);
      expect(state.groups[groupName].tokens).toEqual(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].tokens);
    }
+
    // meta and groups (the fields applyPatch mutates from the recorded
    // patches) must match exactly — this is the load-bearing assertion
    // that the recorded fixture is a valid, replayable patch sequence.
    expect(state.meta).toEqual(recordedFinalState.meta);
    expect(Object.keys(state.groups).sort()).toEqual(Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups).sort());

    // Compare the design signal (each token's $value/$type) exactly.
    // `provenance` is deliberately excluded: it holds event ids generated
    // fresh on each run, so the live recording and this offline replay
    // legitimately assign different ids to the same value (see applyPatch's
    // provenance stamping). We still assert provenance is populated.
    const designTokens = (tokens: Record<string, { $value: unknown; $type: unknown }>) =>
      Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(tokens).map(([k, t]) => [k, { $value: t.$value, $type: t.$type }]));
    for (const groupName of Object.keys(recordedFinalState.groups)) {
      expect(state.groups[groupName].confidence).toEqual(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].confidence);
      expect(designTokens(state.groups[groupName].tokens)).toEqual(
        designTokens(recordedFinalState.groups[groupName].tokens),
      );
      for (const tok of Object.values(state.groups[groupName].tokens)) {
        expect(tok.provenance.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
      }
    }

Replay test passes (3/3), full suite green, build compiles. Let me add an honest gate-results note and commit the live-gate work:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:55:14.814Z
📝 Write GATE_RESULTS.md
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/fixtures/recordedTurns/GATE_RESULTS.md
# Live-API gate results

Status: **partial (1 of 2 personas committed)** — deliberate V0 scope cut under time pressure.

## What's committed

- **`complianceDashboardInterview/`** — a complete 15-turn interview recorded live against `claude-opus-4-8` via the CLI harness (`npm run cli -- --record`). Committed as a fixture and replayed offline by `src/server/__tests__/recordedInterviewReplay.test.ts` (no test makes a live call).
- The dog-groomer persona was also recorded live, but its re-record (against the two live-only turn-protocol bug fixes below) was truncated during a concurrent-write incident and dropped rather than committed in an inconsistent state.

## What the gate established (live)

The live recording path is proven working end-to-end. Observations from the live recording sessions:

- **Live turns succeed** with the three-tool protocol; `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` was non-zero from turn 2 onward (observed `cache_read ≈ 5754`), confirming prompt-cache hygiene.
- **Protocol enforcement works**: a turn that violated the one-`update_beliefs`+one-`interact` rule was recovered by the single corrective retry.
- **Contradiction → clarify**: a deliberately inconsistent answer (a "very corporate and buttoned-up" pivot mid-interview) triggered a clarifying question, as designed.
- **Confidence climbs** to real design signal (the replay test asserts ≥1 token group reaches ≥0.5).

## Bugs the live gate caught (that mocked tests could not)

These were fixed and committed on `main`:

1. Tool-schema derivation 400s (`minimum`/`maximum`, top-level `oneOf`, `minItems`/`maxItems`, and a strict-mode grammar-size limit on `interact`) — `src/contracts/toJsonSchema.ts`.
2. Multi-turn context assembly not strictly alternating roles.
3. The corrective-retry message omitting `tool_result` acks for the rejected `tool_use` blocks.

## Follow-up

Re-record the dog-groomer persona cleanly and re-enable it in the replay test's `describe.each` to restore the full two-persona gate.

The reflog explains it: a git pull --rebase ran (the pull (start)/(pick)/(finish) entries) — it rebased my commits onto origin/main, which flattened the merge commits, and split my Lane 2 amend back into two commits. It started from e55ad8a (my pre-amend Lane 2 SHA, which lives on the remote). No rebase is in progress now, and HEAD (acacf96) is green — but something is syncing main with a remote and rewriting history under me, and there's a new worktree (clever-grothendieck-d6c3b4) from an agent I didn't spawn. Let me gather the facts before touching anything further:

Assistant2026-07-03T20:58:21.923Z

Now it's clear — and it's not damage. The facts: - pull.rebase = true, remote is github.com/claudearchitect/mydsstudio, and local main == origin/main (0 0). - The mystery worktree clever-grothendieck-d6c3b4 is on branch claude/clever-grothendieck-d6c3b4 — that's your polish agent, a remote/cloud agent syncing through origin.

So your polish agent pushes to origin, and pull --rebase folds my local commits on top — which is why the merge commits flattened and my Lane 2 amend split. My work is all preserved, rebased, and already pushed to origin. HEAD acacf96 is green (246 tests + build), and everything is integrated. Let me tidy the merged lane worktrees and re-point the stale tag:

  git worktree remove --force .claude/worktrees/agent-$id 2>/dev/null && echo "removed worktree agent-$id"
done
git worktree prune
git branch -D worktree-agent-a0b4a530f8d841280 worktree-agent-a58fc7cafad55aea5 worktree-agent-ae939203cf4bce4db 2>&1 | head
echo ""

Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:14.434Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:17.448Z

Diagnosis confirmed — and it's a real bug, exactly the one you complained about. The wiring is correct (isPlaceholder: false — these are the real components), but they're stuck in reveal: "absent" with transparent background: at a color proposal, the button's other deps (shape/type/spacing) are still untouched, so "absent beats blurred" forces the whole button absent — hiding the very color the proposal is asking about. Both variants render identically empty.

The fix: proposal variants should render sharp regardless of ambient confidence — a proposal is a focused comparison, not the uncertainty-communicating ambient preview. Let me look at the two files:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:17.913Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/preview/renderComponent.tsx"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:02:17.922Z
1	/**
2	 * renderComponent — Workstream A's real implementation, drop-in for the
3	 * Phase-0 placeholder (`renderComponentPlaceholder` in
4	 * src/contracts/renderComponent.tsx). Same signature:
5	 * `(state: BeliefState, componentId: string) => ReactNode`.
6	 *
7	 * Wraps the exemplar component (src/preview/components/) in a reveal-state
8	 * shell: derives absent/blurred/sharp from the belief state via
9	 * deriveRevealState, and applies the corresponding CSS filter/opacity
10	 * (IMPLEMENTATION.md #5). `data-component` is preserved on the outer node
11	 * because Workstream B's region-select overlay hooks hover/click on that
12	 * attribute (frozen in the Phase-0 placeholder's doc comment) — moving it
13	 * or dropping it breaks region-select.
14	 *
15	 * `data-reveal` is added (not part of the frozen contract, purely additive)
16	 * so dev tooling / tests can assert reveal state without recomputing it.
17	 */
18	import type { RenderComponent } from "@/contracts";
19	import { getManifestEntry } from "@/contracts";
20	import {
21	  REVEAL_CONFIG,
22	  deriveRevealState,
23	  componentConfidence,
24	  blurPxForConfidence,
25	  opacityForConfidence,
26	} from "./revealState";
27	import { COMPONENT_REGISTRY } from "./components";
28	
29	/** Transition applied to every reveal-affecting CSS property so a belief
30	 * patch animates the canvas into focus instead of snapping (V0_PLAN.md
31	 * Workstream A: "smooth CSS transitions on state change"; IMPLEMENTATION.md
32	 * #7: "animate belief patches into the canvas as they arrive"). Kept as a
33	 * single constant so every component's transition stays in lockstep. */
34	export const REVEAL_TRANSITION = "filter 320ms ease, opacity 320ms ease";
35	
36	export const renderComponent: RenderComponent = (state, componentId) => {
37	  const Entry = COMPONENT_REGISTRY[componentId];
38	  const manifestEntry = getManifestEntry(componentId);
39	  const label = manifestEntry?.label ?? componentId;
40	
41	  if (!Entry) {
42	    // Unknown componentId: fail visibly (dev error), same spirit as the
43	    // Phase-0 stub's loud-failure preference — never silently render
44	    // nothing for a manifest entry that has no exemplar yet.
45	    return (
46	      <div data-component={componentId} data-reveal="absent">
47	        Unknown component: {label}
48	      </div>
49	    );
50	  }
51	
52	  const reveal = deriveRevealState(componentId, state, REVEAL_CONFIG);
53	
54	  if (reveal === "absent") {
55	    return (
56	      <div
57	        data-component={componentId}
58	        data-reveal="absent"
59	        style={{
60	          display: "flex",
61	          alignItems: "center",
62	          justifyContent: "center",
63	          minHeight: 48,
64	          minWidth: 96,
65	          padding: 12,
66	          border: "1px dashed #d8d6cc",
67	          borderRadius: 6,
68	          color: "#a8a69c",
69	          fontSize: 12,
70	          fontFamily: "inherit",
71	          transition: REVEAL_TRANSITION,
72	        }}
73	      >
74	        {label}
75	      </div>
76	    );
77	  }
78	
79	  const confidence = componentConfidence(componentId, state, REVEAL_CONFIG);
80	  const blurPx = reveal === "sharp" ? 0 : blurPxForConfidence(confidence, REVEAL_CONFIG);
81	  const opacity = reveal === "sharp" ? 1 : opacityForConfidence(confidence, REVEAL_CONFIG);
82	
83	  return (
84	    <div
85	      data-component={componentId}
86	      data-reveal={reveal}
87	      style={{
88	        display: "inline-flex",
89	        maxWidth: "100%",
90	        filter: blurPx > 0 ? `blur(${blurPx}px)` : "none",
91	        opacity,
92	        transition: REVEAL_TRANSITION,
93	      }}
94	    >
95	      <Entry />
96	    </div>
97	  );
98	};
99
Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:40.701Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:42.655Z

renderComponent renders a labeled dashed box for absent (not the real component), so the color never shows. The cleanest contained fix (no contract change) is in the proposal picker: render each variant against a display-state where the target's dependency groups are bumped to full confidence, so the real button renders sharp with the proposed color + neutral fallbacks. Let me read the picker:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:43.350Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/chat/ProposalPicker.tsx"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:02:43.357Z
1	/**
2	 * Proposal picker — renders a `propose` Interaction's 2-4 variants side by
3	 * side (IMPLEMENTATION.md #3 "Proposals are token patches"). Each variant
4	 * is rendered via `renderComponent(applyPatch(state, patch, throwawayId),
5	 * target)`: the client never interprets a patch, it just applies-and-draws
6	 * it, same renderer as the main preview.
7	 *
8	 * A pick is captured as a chat message describing the choice (never applies
9	 * the patch client-side — single-writer invariant: only the agent's own
10	 * `update_beliefs` on the *next* turn actually commits the change; picking
11	 * "Rounder" here just tells the model that's what the user chose). Always
12	 * offers a "none of these" escape per IMPLEMENTATION.md #3's ask-escape
13	 * rule, mirrored here for proposals.
14	 *
15	 * `renderComponent` is passed in (defaults to the Phase-0 placeholder) so
16	 * Workstream A's real implementation drops in without this component
17	 * changing (same signature, per contracts/renderComponent.tsx).
18	 */
19	"use client";
20	
21	import { useMemo } from "react";
22	import {
23	  applyPatch,
24	  renderComponentPlaceholder,
25	  type BeliefState,
26	  type ProposeInteraction,
27	  type ProposalVariant,
28	  type RenderComponent,
29	} from "@/contracts";
30	
31	export interface ProposalPickerProps {
32	  state: BeliefState;
33	  interaction: ProposeInteraction;
34	  onPick: (variant: ProposalVariant) => void;
35	  onNoneOfThese: () => void;
36	  disabled?: boolean;
37	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
38	}
39	
40	export function ProposalPicker({
41	  state,
42	  interaction,
43	  onPick,
44	  onNoneOfThese,
45	  disabled,
46	  renderComponent = renderComponentPlaceholder,
47	}: ProposalPickerProps) {
48	  // A throwaway id — this preview render is never appended to state.events;
49	  // applyPatch's eventId is only used for provenance stamping on the
50	  // scratch copy discarded after this render (contracts/applyPatch.ts:
51	  // "caller owns event-id generation and event-log appends").
52	  const variantStates = useMemo(
53	    () =>
54	      interaction.variants.map((variant) => ({
55	        variant,
56	        previewState: applyPatch(state, variant.patch, `preview-${variant.id}`),
57	      })),
58	    [state, interaction.variants],
59	  );
60	
61	  return (
62	    <div
63	      className="rounded-app-lg bg-app-bg-raised p-3 shadow-app-card"
64	      data-testid="proposal-picker"
65	      data-axis={interaction.axis.join(",")}
66	      data-target={interaction.target}
67	    >
68	      <p className="mb-3 text-sm text-app-text">{interaction.caption}</p>
69	      <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
70	        {variantStates.map(({ variant, previewState }) => (
71	          <button
72	            key={variant.id}
73	            type="button"
74	            disabled={disabled}
75	            onClick={() => onPick(variant)}
76	            className="flex flex-col items-center gap-2 rounded-app-md bg-app-paper p-3 text-left shadow-app-edge transition-shadow hover:shadow-app-focus disabled:opacity-40"
77	            data-testid={`proposal-variant-${variant.id}`}
78	          >
79	            <div className="ds-preview-root flex w-full items-center justify-center rounded-app-sm p-2">
80	              {renderComponent(previewState, interaction.target)}
81	            </div>
82	            <span className="text-xs font-medium" style={{ color: "#2b2a26" }}>
83	              {variant.caption}
84	            </span>
85	          </button>
86	        ))}
87	      </div>
88	      <button
89	        type="button"
90	        disabled={disabled}
91	        onClick={onNoneOfThese}
92	        className="mt-3 text-xs text-app-text-muted underline decoration-dotted hover:text-app-text-secondary disabled:opacity-40"
93	        data-testid="proposal-none-of-these"
94	      >
95	        None of these
96	      </button>
97	    </div>
98	  );
99	}
100
Assistant2026-07-03T21:02:59.778Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:03:00.269Z

I'll bump the target's dependency groups to full confidence in a display-only copy so the real component renders sharp. Let me check the manifest API to get those groups:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:03:01.011Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/contracts/componentManifest.ts"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:03:01.017Z
1	/**
2	 * Component manifest — componentId -> dotted token-ref dependencies
3	 * (IMPLEMENTATION.md #5). Powers both region-select resolution (Workstream
4	 * B) and progressive-reveal confidence derivation (Workstream A). Frozen
5	 * set for V0: button, card, input, heading, badge, nav.
6	 *
7	 * componentId naming convention: "<family>.<variant>", e.g.
8	 * "button.primary". Families with only one exemplar still use the dotted
9	 * form for consistency ("card.default") so region-select and the manifest
10	 * never need two id shapes.
11	 */
12	
13	export interface ComponentManifestEntry {
14	  componentId: string;
15	  /** Human label for dev tooling (fixture switcher, inspector panel). */
16	  label: string;
17	  /** Dotted token refs this component's rendering depends on, e.g.
18	   * "color.primary". Order is not meaningful. */
19	  tokenGroups: string[];
20	}
21	
22	export const COMPONENT_MANIFEST: ComponentManifestEntry[] = [
23	  {
24	    componentId: "button.primary",
25	    label: "Primary button",
26	    tokenGroups: [
27	      "color.primary",
28	      "color.onPrimary",
29	      "shape.radius",
30	      "typography.label",
31	      "spacing.inset",
32	    ],
33	  },
34	  {
35	    componentId: "card.default",
36	    label: "Card",
37	    tokenGroups: [
38	      "color.surface",
39	      "color.border",
40	      "shape.radius",
41	      "elevation.card",
42	      "spacing.inset",
43	    ],
44	  },
45	  {
46	    componentId: "input.text",
47	    label: "Text input",
48	    tokenGroups: [
49	      "color.surface",
50	      "color.border",
51	      "color.text",
52	      "shape.radius",
53	      "typography.body",
54	      "spacing.inset",
55	    ],
56	  },
57	  {
58	    componentId: "heading.default",
59	    label: "Heading",
60	    tokenGroups: ["color.text", "typography.heading"],
61	  },
62	  {
63	    componentId: "badge.default",
64	    label: "Badge",
65	    tokenGroups: [
66	      "color.accent",
67	      "color.onAccent",
68	      "shape.radiusPill",
69	      "typography.label",
70	    ],
71	  },
72	  {
73	    componentId: "nav.default",
74	    label: "Nav bar",
75	    tokenGroups: [
76	      "color.surface",
77	      "color.border",
78	      "color.text",
79	      "typography.label",
80	      "spacing.inset",
81	    ],
82	  },
83	];
84	
85	export function getManifestEntry(
86	  componentId: string,
87	): ComponentManifestEntry | undefined {
88	  return COMPONENT_MANIFEST.find((e) => e.componentId === componentId);
89	}
90	
91	/** Resolves a component's tokensInScope map (dotted ref -> current resolved
92	 * value) against a token-value lookup — used by Workstream B's region-select
93	 * handler. Kept here (not in a workstream dir) since both A and B need the
94	 * exact same lookup semantics. */
95	export function tokensInScopeFor(
96	  componentId: string,
97	  resolve: (dottedRef: string) => string | number | undefined,
98	): Record<string, string | number> {
99	  const entry = getManifestEntry(componentId);
100	  if (!entry) return {};
101	  const out: Record<string, string | number> = {};
102	  for (const ref of entry.tokenGroups) {
103	    const v = resolve(ref);
104	    if (v !== undefined) out[ref] = v;
105	  }
106	  return out;
107	}
108
Assistant2026-07-03T21:03:21.144Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:03:21.620Z

The manifest gives me each component's dependency groups. Implementing the display-only "sharpen" in the picker:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:03:30.198Z
✏️ Edit ProposalPicker.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/chat/ProposalPicker.tsx
−
import { useMemo } from "react";
import {
  applyPatch,
  renderComponentPlaceholder,
  type BeliefState,
  type ProposeInteraction,
  type ProposalVariant,
  type RenderComponent,
} from "@/contracts";
+
import { useMemo } from "react";
import {
  applyPatch,
  getManifestEntry,
  parseTokenRef,
  renderComponentPlaceholder,
  type BeliefState,
  type ProposeInteraction,
  type ProposalVariant,
  type RenderComponent,
  type TokenGroupName,
} from "@/contracts";

/** Confidence forced onto a proposal target's dependency groups for the
 * picker's display render only (never written to real state). */
const SHARP_DISPLAY_CONFIDENCE = 0.95;

/**
 * Display-only transform: raise the target component's dependency groups to
 * full confidence so the real component renders SHARP in the picker,
 * regardless of ambient session confidence. A proposal is a *focused
 * comparison* of the probed axis — reveal-state uncertainty belongs to the
 * ambient preview, not here. Without this, a color proposal made before
 * shape/typography/spacing are decided renders the button "absent" (a
 * colorless placeholder), hiding the very axis being compared. Unset tokens
 * fall back to the components' neutral defaults; the variant's own patch
 * supplies the probed value. Not written to state.events or persisted.
 */
function sharpenForTarget(s: BeliefState, target: string): BeliefState {
  const entry = getManifestEntry(target);
  if (!entry) return s;
  const groups = { ...s.groups };
  for (const ref of entry.tokenGroups) {
    const group = parseTokenRef(ref).group as TokenGroupName;
    const existing = groups[group];
    groups[group] = {
      confidence: Math.max(existing?.confidence ?? 0, SHARP_DISPLAY_CONFIDENCE),
      tokens: existing?.tokens ?? {},
    };
  }
  return { ...s, groups };
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:03:30.215Z

✏️ Edit ProposalPicker.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/chat/ProposalPicker.tsx
−
      interaction.variants.map((variant) => ({
        variant,
        previewState: applyPatch(state, variant.patch, `preview-${variant.id}`),
      })),
+
      interaction.variants.map((variant) => ({
        variant,
        previewState: sharpenForTarget(
          applyPatch(state, variant.patch, `preview-${variant.id}`),
          interaction.target,
        ),
      })),

Assistant2026-07-03T21:04:57.647Z

Found the actual root cause: --ds-color-primary is empty on the picker's .ds-preview-root — both buttons fall back to grey. The ProposalPicker wraps variants in .ds-preview-root but never applies resolveTokens(previewState) as the CSS variables, so the variant colors never reach the DOM. Let me see how the main PreviewPane does it, to mirror it exactly:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:05:54.252Z

Assistant2026-07-03T21:05:56.787Z

Found the core visual bug. There are two preview implementations: Workstream A's src/preview/PreviewPanel.tsx correctly applies resolveTokens(state) as CSS variables — but the shell actually mounts Workstream B's src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx, which never injects the --ds-* variables. So the entire preview (and the proposal picker) renders with fallback neutrals, never the belief state's real colors. That's why both buttons are grey. Fixing both surfaces to inject the resolved tokens:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:05:58.740Z
✏️ Edit PreviewPane.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx
−
import { COMPONENT_MANIFEST, type BeliefState, type RenderComponent } from "@/contracts";
+
import { COMPONENT_MANIFEST, resolveTokens, type BeliefState, type RenderComponent } from "@/contracts";

−

          className="ds-preview-root h-full w-full rounded-app-lg p-6 shadow-app-paper"
          data-testid="ds-preview-root"
        >
+
        <div
          className="ds-preview-root h-full w-full rounded-app-lg p-6 shadow-app-paper"
          data-testid="ds-preview-root"
          style={resolveTokens(displayState) as React.CSSProperties}
        >

{
  "serverId": "ce1948d1-2fc9-4773-978a-54a08d6750c4",
  "expression": "(async () => {\n  const send = async (text) => {\n    const inp = document.querySelector('input[type=\"text\"], textarea');\n    if(!inp) return 'no input';\n    const setter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype,'value')?.set || Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype,'value')?.set;\n    setter.call(inp, text); inp.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true}));\n    await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,50));\n    const b=[...document.querySelectorAll('button')].find(x=>x.textContent.trim()==='Send');\n    if(b && !b.disabled) b.click();\n    return 'sent';\n  };\n  const results=[];\n  for(let i=0;i<3;i++){ results.push(await send('sounds good, yes')); await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,900)); }\n  return results;\n})()"
}

  "expression": "(() => {\n  const modal = document.querySelector('[data-testid=\"export-modal\"]');\n  if(!modal) return {noModal:true};\n  const raw = modal.innerText || '';\n  // also try to find any element whose text contains YAML front matter marker\n  const hasFront = raw.includes('---') || raw.includes('tokens');\n  return { innerTextLen: raw.length, sample: raw.slice(0, 800), hasFrontMatterish: hasFront };\n})()"
}

localStorage restore works — after reload, the welcome screen shows "RESUME YOUR LAST SESSION — a booking app for dog groomers — Saved 1 min ago" with a Resume button.

Final acceptance is complete. Now committing the two critical fixes I made during verification (without them the entire preview rendered grey — a real visual failure):

Assistant2026-07-03T21:10:08.485Z
$ Bash
Commit the two visual fixes
git status --short
echo "=== commit the visual fixes ==="
git add src/shell/chat/ProposalPicker.tsx src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx
git commit -q -m "Render real belief-state colors in preview + make proposals visually distinct

Two integration bugs found during final acceptance:
1. Neither the shell PreviewPane nor the ProposalPicker injected
   resolveTokens(state) as --ds-* CSS variables, so every preview
   component fell back to neutral greys instead of the belief state's
   real colors. Both now apply resolveTokens to their .ds-preview-root.
2. Proposal variants rendered 'absent' (colorless placeholder) when the
   probed axis (e.g. color) was decided before the component's other
   dependency groups — hiding the very axis being compared. ProposalPicker
   now sharpens the target's dependency groups for display so variants
   render the real component with the proposed value (muted green vs bold
   teal now show as actual colored buttons).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git log --oneline -1

Assistant2026-07-03T21:10:47.034Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:10:59.278Z

✅ V0 is complete and verified

HEAD 8c6949e, tagged v0-complete — 246 tests + build green, no test touches the live API.

Final acceptance — full happy path, verified live in the browser (demo mode, no key)

I drove the real app end to end: 1. Welcome → Start (demo) — keyless, matches the studio-shell spec (dark warm chrome, terracotta, Claude-style mark) 2. Interview — the model owns its opening question; my answer captured the product into the header 3. Visual proposal — "muted green vs bold teal" render as actual colored buttons side by side 4. Pick applies → the preview resolves into a real design system: green button, "Book your groomer" card, "Pet's name" input, green badge, "Maple & Co." nav — all in the chosen palette at 10px radius 5. Region-aware follow-up ("anything about the primary button that feels off, now that you can see it?") 6. Export → valid design.md: YAML front matter parses, primary: '#5b7f5e' traces exactly to my pick, low-confidence groups marked inferred 7. Reload → Resume — localStorage restore works 8. Debug inspector collapsed + labeled ✓; controls show real color swatches ✓

Two real bugs I caught and fixed during acceptance

Both would have sunk the demo — the whole point is visual reasoning, and the preview was rendering grey fallbacks: 1. Neither shell preview surface injected resolveTokens() as --ds-* variables → every component ignored the belief-state colors. Fixed in PreviewPane + ProposalPicker. 2. Proposal variants rendered absent/colorless when the probed axis was decided before other dependencies. Now the picker sharpens the target for display, so variants show the real component in the proposed color.

Deliverables on main

  • Working app (demo + live modes), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/DESIGN.md, README, WRITEUP, DEPLOY, DEMO_SCRIPT
  • Live gate: compliance-dashboard interview + offline replay test (1 of 2 personas, documented in GATE_RESULTS.md)
  • Revert points: v0-phase0 → v0-integrated → v0-complete

Honest follow-ups (not blockers)

  • Live gate: re-record the dog-groomer persona to restore the 2-persona gate (dropped after the concurrent-write truncation)
  • Lead fast-follow: the "vary again / go bolder" variation control
  • Human TODOs: fill WRITEUP.md "Time spent"; run an actual vercel deploy per DEPLOY.md

The app is running at localhost:3000 if you want to explore it. That completes the V0 build plan.

}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:12:28.263Z
1	/**
2	 * ChatPanel — the left-column conversation surface (V0_PLAN.md Workstream B
3	 * "Chat panel" + "Session UI shell" left column). Composes the message
4	 * list, the live ask/propose interaction, quick replies, the proposal
5	 * picker, and the always-present free-text input.
6	 */
7	"use client";
8	
9	import type {
10	  BeliefState,
11	  Interaction,
12	  NormalizedMessage,
13	  ProposalVariant,
14	  QuickReply,
15	  RenderComponent,
16	} from "@/contracts";
17	import { normalizeChatMessage } from "../messages/normalize";
18	import type { TranscriptEntry } from "../state/transcript";
19	import { StudioLogo } from "../Logo";
20	import { MessageList } from "./MessageList";
21	import { ChatInput } from "./ChatInput";
22	
23	export interface ChatPanelProps {
24	  transcript: TranscriptEntry[];
25	  liveInteraction: Interaction | null;
26	  beliefState: BeliefState;
27	  isStreaming: boolean;
28	  streamingText: string;
29	  onSendMessage: (message: NormalizedMessage) => void;
30	  onRetry: () => void;
31	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
32	}
33	
34	export function ChatPanel({
35	  transcript,
36	  liveInteraction,
37	  beliefState,
38	  isStreaming,
39	  streamingText,
40	  onSendMessage,
41	  onRetry,
42	  renderComponent,
43	}: ChatPanelProps) {
44	  function handleFreeText(text: string) {
45	    onSendMessage(normalizeChatMessage(text));
46	  }
47	
48	  function handleQuickReply(reply: QuickReply) {
49	    onSendMessage(normalizeChatMessage(reply.label));
50	  }
51	
52	  function handleProposalPick(variant: ProposalVariant, interaction: Interaction) {
53	    if (interaction.mode !== "propose") return;
54	    onSendMessage(
55	      normalizeChatMessage(
56	        `user picked "${variant.caption}" for the ${interaction.axis.join(", ")} proposal on ${interaction.target}`,
57	      ),
58	    );
59	  }
60	
61	  function handleNoneOfThese(interaction: Interaction) {
62	    if (interaction.mode !== "propose") return;
63	    onSendMessage(
64	      normalizeChatMessage(
65	        `none of these feel right for ${interaction.axis.join(", ")} — user wants different options`,
66	      ),
67	    );
68	  }
69	
70	  return (
71	    <div className="flex h-full flex-col" data-testid="chat-panel">
72	      <header className="flex items-center gap-2.5 border-b border-app-border px-4 py-3">
73	        <StudioLogo />
74	        <div>
75	          <p className="text-sm font-semibold tracking-tight text-app-text">MyDS Studio</p>
76	          <p className="text-xs text-app-text-muted">interview session</p>
77	        </div>
78	      </header>
79	
80	      <MessageList
81	        transcript={transcript}
82	        liveInteraction={liveInteraction}
83	        beliefState={beliefState}
84	        isStreaming={isStreaming}
85	        streamingText={streamingText}
86	        disabled={isStreaming}
87	        onQuickReply={handleQuickReply}
88	        onProposalPick={handleProposalPick}
89	        onNoneOfThese={handleNoneOfThese}
90	        onRetry={onRetry}
91	        renderComponent={renderComponent}
92	      />
93	
94	      <div className="border-t border-app-border p-3">
95	        <ChatInput
96	          onSubmit={handleFreeText}
97	          disabled={isStreaming}
98	          placeholder={isStreaming ? "Waiting for response…" : "Type a message… (always available)"}
99	        />
100	      </div>
101	    </div>
102	  );
103	}
104
