Assistant2026-07-03T21:12:43.929Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/chat/MessageList.tsx"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:12:43.933Z
1	/**
2	 * Renders the transcript: past user messages, past agent interactions
3	 * (collapsed to their question/caption text once superseded), the *current*
4	 * live interaction (full ask/propose card), and any error banners.
5	 */
6	"use client";
7	
8	import type {
9	  BeliefState,
10	  Interaction,
11	  NormalizedMessage,
12	  ProposalVariant,
13	  QuickReply,
14	  RenderComponent,
15	} from "@/contracts";
16	import type { TranscriptEntry } from "../state/transcript";
17	import { TurnErrorBanner } from "../feedback/TurnErrorBanner";
18	import { QuickReplies } from "./QuickReplies";
19	import { ProposalPicker } from "./ProposalPicker";
20	
21	export interface MessageListProps {
22	  transcript: TranscriptEntry[];
23	  liveInteraction: Interaction | null;
24	  beliefState: BeliefState;
25	  isStreaming: boolean;
26	  streamingText: string;
27	  disabled: boolean;
28	  onQuickReply: (reply: QuickReply) => void;
29	  onProposalPick: (variant: ProposalVariant, interaction: Interaction) => void;
30	  onNoneOfThese: (interaction: Interaction) => void;
31	  onRetry: () => void;
32	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
33	}
34	
35	export function MessageList({
36	  transcript,
37	  liveInteraction,
38	  beliefState,
39	  isStreaming,
40	  streamingText,
41	  disabled,
42	  onQuickReply,
43	  onProposalPick,
44	  onNoneOfThese,
45	  onRetry,
46	  renderComponent,
47	}: MessageListProps) {
48	  return (
49	    <div className="flex-1 space-y-3 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-4" data-testid="message-list">
50	      {transcript.map((entry) => (
51	        <TranscriptRow key={entry.id} entry={entry} onRetry={onRetry} />
52	      ))}
53	
54	      {isStreaming && (
55	        <div
56	          className="flex max-w-[85%] items-center gap-2 rounded-app-md bg-app-bg-raised px-3 py-2 text-sm text-app-text-secondary shadow-app-card"
57	          data-testid="streaming-indicator"
58	        >
59	          <ThinkingDots />
60	          <span>{streamingText || "thinking…"}</span>
61	        </div>
62	      )}
63	
64	      {!isStreaming && liveInteraction && (
65	        <LiveInteractionCard
66	          interaction={liveInteraction}
67	          beliefState={beliefState}
68	          disabled={disabled}
69	          onQuickReply={onQuickReply}
70	          onProposalPick={onProposalPick}
71	          onNoneOfThese={onNoneOfThese}
72	          renderComponent={renderComponent}
73	        />
74	      )}
75	    </div>
76	  );
77	}
78	
79	function TranscriptRow({
80	  entry,
81	  onRetry,
82	}: {
83	  entry: TranscriptEntry;
84	  onRetry: () => void;
85	}) {
86	  if (entry.kind === "userMessage") {
87	    return (
88	      <div
89	        className="ml-auto max-w-[85%] rounded-app-md bg-app-accent px-3 py-2 text-sm text-white shadow-app-card"
90	        data-testid="transcript-user-message"
91	      >
92	        {messageText(entry.message)}
93	      </div>
94	    );
95	  }
96	
97	  if (entry.kind === "agentInteraction") {
98	    return (
99	      <div
100	        className="max-w-[85%] rounded-app-md bg-app-bg-raised px-3 py-2 text-sm text-app-text shadow-app-card"
101	        data-testid="transcript-agent-interaction"
102	      >
103	        {entry.interaction.mode === "ask" ? entry.interaction.question : entry.interaction.caption}
104	      </div>
105	    );
106	  }
107	
108	  // agentError
109	  return <TurnErrorBanner code={entry.code} message={entry.message} onRetry={onRetry} />;
110	}
111	
112	function LiveInteractionCard({
113	  interaction,
114	  beliefState,
115	  disabled,
116	  onQuickReply,
117	  onProposalPick,
118	  onNoneOfThese,
119	  renderComponent,
120	}: {
121	  interaction: Interaction;
122	  beliefState: BeliefState;
123	  disabled: boolean;
124	  onQuickReply: (reply: QuickReply) => void;
125	  onProposalPick: (variant: ProposalVariant, interaction: Interaction) => void;
126	  onNoneOfThese: (interaction: Interaction) => void;
127	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
128	}) {
129	  if (interaction.mode === "ask") {
130	    return (
131	      <div className="flex flex-col gap-2" data-testid="live-interaction-ask">
132	        <div className="max-w-[85%] rounded-app-md bg-app-bg-raised px-3 py-2 text-sm text-app-text shadow-app-card">
133	          {interaction.question}
134	        </div>
135	        <QuickReplies
136	          quickReplies={interaction.quickReplies}
137	          onPick={onQuickReply}
138	          disabled={disabled}
139	        />
140	      </div>
141	    );
142	  }
143	
144	  return (
145	    <ProposalPicker
146	      state={beliefState}
147	      interaction={interaction}
148	      onPick={(variant) => onProposalPick(variant, interaction)}
149	      onNoneOfThese={() => onNoneOfThese(interaction)}
150	      disabled={disabled}
151	      renderComponent={renderComponent}
152	    />
153	  );
154	}
155	
156	function messageText(message: NormalizedMessage): string {
157	  return message.text;
158	}
159	
160	function ThinkingDots() {
161	  return (
162	    <span className="flex gap-0.5" aria-hidden>
163	      <span className="h-1.5 w-1.5 animate-pulse rounded-full bg-app-text-muted" />
164	      <span className="h-1.5 w-1.5 animate-pulse rounded-full bg-app-text-muted [animation-delay:150ms]" />
165	      <span className="h-1.5 w-1.5 animate-pulse rounded-full bg-app-text-muted [animation-delay:300ms]" />
166	    </span>
167	  );
168	}
169
Assistant2026-07-03T21:12:57.524Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:12:58.545Z

The duplication is clear: MessageList renders every agentInteraction transcript entry (line 50-52) and the current liveInteraction card (line 64-74) — and the current interaction is present in both. The design intent (per the header comment) is that the transcript holds past interactions and liveInteraction is the current one. Let me see how useSession builds the transcript:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:12:59.464Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/turn/useSession.ts"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:12:59.476Z
1	/**
2	 * useSession — the shell's central orchestrator. Owns:
3	 *  - the current BeliefState (read-only outside of applying an agent's
4	 *    patch — single-writer invariant: only `applyPatch`, called here with
5	 *    the agent's own patch/eventId, ever changes it)
6	 *  - the transcript (UI history)
7	 *  - turn lifecycle (one in-flight turn at a time, streaming deltas, error
8	 *    banner + retry)
9	 *  - localStorage snapshot/restore
10	 *  - the outgoing normalized-message log for the dev inspector
11	 *
12	 * This hook does NOT build NormalizedMessages itself — callers (chat input,
13	 * region popover, controls) construct them via src/shell/messages/normalize
14	 * and hand them to `sendMessage`. This keeps "how a channel phrases itself"
15	 * separate from "how a turn is dispatched and applied".
16	 */
17	"use client";
18	
19	import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
20	import type { BeliefState, Interaction, NormalizedMessage } from "@/contracts";
21	import { emptyBeliefState } from "@fixtures/beliefStates";
22	import type { TurnAgent, TurnAgentResult } from "./turnAgent";
23	import {
24	  nextTranscriptId,
25	  type TranscriptEntry,
26	} from "../state/transcript";
27	import { restoreSession, saveSession, type SessionStorageMode } from "../state/sessionStorage";
28	
29	export interface UseSessionOptions {
30	  agent: TurnAgent;
31	  /** Which mode this session's persistence is namespaced under ("live" vs
32	   * "demo" — see sessionStorage.ts's header comment for why a shared key
33	   * across modes was a bug: it let one mode's restored transcript get
34	   * replayed into the other mode's turn source). */
35	  storageMode: SessionStorageMode;
36	  /** Skip the localStorage restore attempt (useful in tests). */
37	  disablePersistence?: boolean;
38	  /** Automatically play the agent's kickoff turn on mount if there's no
39	   * restored session and no interaction yet. */
40	  autoKickoff?: boolean;
41	}
42	
43	export interface UseSessionResult {
44	  beliefState: BeliefState;
45	  transcript: TranscriptEntry[];
46	  interaction: Interaction | null;
47	  /** Outgoing NormalizedMessages this session has sent, most-recent-last —
48	   * feeds the dev inspector panel. */
49	  outgoingMessages: NormalizedMessage[];
50	  isStreaming: boolean;
51	  streamingText: string;
52	  error: { code: string; message: string } | null;
53	  sendMessage: (message: NormalizedMessage) => Promise<void>;
54	  retry: () => void;
55	  agentExhausted: boolean;
56	  /** True once the agent has signaled confident completion (V0_PLAN.md
57	   * Phase 2 "Completion state") — drives the Export CTA. Reset to false on
58	   * every new turn until that turn's result says otherwise, so it always
59	   * reflects the *latest* turn, not "ever completed this session". */
60	  completed: boolean;
61	}
62	
63	export function useSession({
64	  agent,
65	  storageMode,
66	  disablePersistence,
67	  autoKickoff = true,
68	}: UseSessionOptions): UseSessionResult {
69	  // Always initialize to the empty/SSR-safe state, matching what the server
70	  // renders (localStorage doesn't exist server-side) — restoring a real
71	  // snapshot happens in the effect below, strictly after mount. Seeding
72	  // this initializer from localStorage directly would make the client's
73	  // first render diverge from the server-rendered HTML and trigger a
74	  // hydration mismatch (React re-does the whole tree client-side to
75	  // recover, which is correct but noisy and defeats SSR).
76	  const [beliefState, setBeliefState] = useState<BeliefState>(emptyBeliefState);
77	  const [transcript, setTranscript] = useState<TranscriptEntry[]>([]);
78	  const [restoredFromStorage, setRestoredFromStorage] = useState(false);
79	  const [interaction, setInteraction] = useState<Interaction | null>(null);
80	  const [outgoingMessages, setOutgoingMessages] = useState<NormalizedMessage[]>([]);
81	  const [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);
82	  const [streamingText, setStreamingText] = useState("");
83	  const [error, setError] = useState<{ code: string; message: string } | null>(null);
84	  const [completed, setCompleted] = useState(false);
85	
86	  const lastMessageRef = useRef<NormalizedMessage | null>(null);
87	  const kickedOffRef = useRef(false);
88	
89	  const runTurn = useCallback(
90	    async (message: NormalizedMessage | null) => {
91	      setIsStreaming(true);
92	      setStreamingText("");
93	      setError(null);
94	      lastMessageRef.current = message;
95	      // Not resetting `completed` here: a mid-flight turn (e.g. the user
96	      // sends one more nudge after completion) should keep showing the
97	      // Export CTA until the *new* turn's result says otherwise — flipping
98	      // it off during the in-flight window would flash the CTA away and
99	      // back for no reason. `completed` is set from `result.completed` once
100	      // the turn settles, whatever that turns out to be.
101	
102	      let result: TurnAgentResult;
103	      try {
104	        result = await agent.runTurn(message, beliefState, {
105	          onDelta: (chunk) => setStreamingText((prev) => prev + chunk),
106	        });
107	      } catch (err) {
108	        result = {
109	          kind: "error",
110	          code: "unknown",
111	          message: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
112	        };
113	      }
114	
115	      setIsStreaming(false);
116	
117	      if (result.kind === "error") {
118	        setError({ code: result.code, message: result.message });
119	        setTranscript((prev) => [
120	          ...prev,
121	          {
122	            kind: "agentError",
123	            id: nextTranscriptId("err"),
124	            code: result.code,
125	            message: result.message,
126	            ts: new Date().toISOString(),
127	          },
128	        ]);
129	        return;
130	      }
131	
132	      setBeliefState(result.beliefState);
133	      setInteraction(result.interaction);
134	      setCompleted(Boolean(result.completed));
135	      setTranscript((prev) => [
136	        ...prev,
137	        {
138	          kind: "agentInteraction",
139	          id: nextTranscriptId("turn"),
140	          interaction: result.interaction,
141	          deltaText: streamingText || undefined,
142	          ts: new Date().toISOString(),
143	          completed: result.completed,
144	        },
145	      ]);
146	    },
147	    // streamingText intentionally excluded: reading it inside the async
148	    // closure would race with setStreamingText from onDelta; the transcript
149	    // entry's deltaText is best-effort and not load-bearing for the gate.
150	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
151	    [agent, beliefState],
152	  );
153	
154	  const sendMessage = useCallback(
155	    async (message: NormalizedMessage) => {
156	      if (isStreaming) return; // one in-flight turn at a time
157	      setOutgoingMessages((prev) => [...prev, message]);
158	      setTranscript((prev) => [
159	        ...prev,
160	        {
161	          kind: "userMessage",
162	          id: nextTranscriptId("msg"),
163	          message,
164	          ts: new Date().toISOString(),
165	        },
166	      ]);
167	      await runTurn(message);
168	    },
169	    [isStreaming, runTurn],
170	  );
171	
172	  const retry = useCallback(() => {
173	    if (isStreaming) return;
174	    void runTurn(lastMessageRef.current);
175	  }, [isStreaming, runTurn]);
176	
177	  // Post-mount localStorage restore (see the state-init comment above for
178	  // why this can't happen in the useState initializer). Runs once; a
179	  // restored transcript/belief state is only applied if there actually was
180	  // one to restore — otherwise this just flips `restoredFromStorage` so the
181	  // kickoff effect below knows it's safe to proceed with a fresh session.
182	  // Deferred via queueMicrotask for the same react-hooks/set-state-in-effect
183	  // reason as the kickoff effect below — this keeps the effect body itself
184	  // free of direct setState calls while still resolving before paint.
185	  useEffect(() => {
186	    queueMicrotask(() => {
187	      if (disablePersistence) {
188	        setRestoredFromStorage(true);
189	        return;
190	      }
191	      const restored = restoreSession(storageMode);
192	      // Only treat this as "a session to resume" if it actually has
193	      // conversation history. A session persisted before its kickoff turn
194	      // ever resolved (e.g. the tab was closed mid-kickoff) parses fine but
195	      // has an empty transcript; treating *that* as "restored" would
196	      // permanently skip the kickoff turn (kickedOffRef latched true) and
197	      // leave the session stuck with no opening question forever. Only a
198	      // non-empty transcript is real history.
199	      //
200	      // `storageMode` namespaces this lookup so a demo-mode mount's saved
201	      // state is never read back by a live-mode mount (or vice versa) —
202	      // sessionStorage.ts's header comment has the full history of the bug
203	      // this fixes: a shared key let one mode's stale/empty transcript get
204	      // "restored" into the other mode's session, latching kickedOffRef and
205	      // leaving the shell stuck with no way to progress.
206	      if (restored && restored.transcript.length > 0) {
207	        setBeliefState(restored.beliefState);
208	        setTranscript(restored.transcript);
209	        // A restored session already has its own history — never replay
210	        // the scripted kickoff turn on top of it.
211	        kickedOffRef.current = true;
212	      }
213	      setRestoredFromStorage(true);
214	    });
215	  }, [disablePersistence, storageMode]);
216	
217	  // Kickoff turn: a fresh session (no interaction yet, nothing restored)
218	  // asks the opening question with no user message (IMPLEMENTATION.md #3 /
219	  // V0_PLAN.md Workstream C "Kickoff turn"). Guarded so it fires once, and
220	  // only after the restore attempt above has resolved (so a
221	  // to-be-restored session never gets a duplicate scripted kickoff turn
222	  // played on top of it). `runTurn` sets state synchronously at its start
223	  // (setIsStreaming(true)); queueing it as a microtask keeps this effect's
224	  // own body free of direct setState calls (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect)
225	  // while still starting the kickoff turn right after mount.
226	  useEffect(() => {
227	    if (!autoKickoff) return;
228	    if (!restoredFromStorage) return;
229	    if (kickedOffRef.current) return;
230	    if (interaction !== null) return;
231	    if (isStreaming) return;
232	    kickedOffRef.current = true;
233	    queueMicrotask(() => void runTurn(null));
234	    // runTurn/interaction/isStreaming intentionally excluded below: this
235	    // effect must fire once on mount (after restore resolves), not re-run
236	    // whenever a turn changes `interaction`/`isStreaming` (read here only
237	    // as guards); `kickedOffRef` is the actual one-shot guard.
238	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
239	  }, [autoKickoff, restoredFromStorage]);
240	
241	  // Persist on every settled change.
242	  useEffect(() => {
243	    if (disablePersistence) return;
244	    saveSession(storageMode, beliefState, transcript);
245	  }, [beliefState, transcript, disablePersistence, storageMode]);
246	
247	  return {
248	    beliefState,
249	    transcript,
250	    interaction,
251	    outgoingMessages,
252	    isStreaming,
253	    streamingText,
254	    error,
255	    sendMessage,
256	    retry,
257	    agentExhausted: agent.exhausted,
258	    completed,
259	  };
260	}
261
Assistant2026-07-03T21:14:40.255Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:14:42.198Z

Root cause found. In useSession (lines 132–145), every settled turn sets interaction = result.interaction and pushes that same interaction into the transcript as an agentInteraction — so MessageList renders it twice (transcript row + live card). The fix: skip the transcript row for the interaction that's currently shown as the live card. Let me start the fixes — chat first (duplicate + redundant logo):

Assistant2026-07-03T21:14:45.677Z
✏️ Edit MessageList.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/chat/MessageList.tsx
−
      {transcript.map((entry) => (
        <TranscriptRow key={entry.id} entry={entry} onRetry={onRetry} />
      ))}
+
      {transcript.map((entry) => {
        // The current interaction is rendered once as the live card below, so
        // skip its duplicate transcript row (they hold the same interaction
        // object). During streaming the live card is hidden, so keep the row
        // for conversation context — no duplicate arises then.
        if (
          !isStreaming &&
          entry.kind === "agentInteraction" &&
          entry.interaction === liveInteraction
        ) {
          return null;
        }
        return <TranscriptRow key={entry.id} entry={entry} onRetry={onRetry} />;
      })}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:14:45.708Z

62:    expect(node?.getAttribute("data-reveal")).toBe("absent");
65:  it("renders data-reveal='sharp' at 0.95 confidence", () => {

71:  it("renders data-reveal='absent' for the 0.1 fixture (untouched dependency groups)", () => {

84:  it("button.primary renders real button chrome: two buttons, real labels, no placeholder text", () => {
88:    const labels = Array.from(buttons).map((b) => b.textContent);
89:    expect(labels.some((t) => /book/i.test(t ?? ""))).toBe(true);

Absent tests only assert the data-reveal attribute (which I'll preserve), so changing the absent visual to render the real component is safe. Updating the absent branch to render the real component as a muted skeleton:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:15:43.315Z
✏️ Edit renderComponent.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/preview/renderComponent.tsx
−
  const reveal = deriveRevealState(componentId, state, REVEAL_CONFIG);

  if (reveal === "absent") {
    return (
      <div
        data-component={componentId}
        data-reveal="absent"
        style={{
          display: "flex",
          alignItems: "center",
          justifyContent: "center",
          minHeight: 48,
          minWidth: 96,
          padding: 12,
          border: "1px dashed #d8d6cc",
          borderRadius: 6,
          color: "#a8a69c",
          fontSize: 12,
          fontFamily: "inherit",
          transition: REVEAL_TRANSITION,
        }}
      >
        {label}
      </div>
    );
  }
+
  const reveal = deriveRevealState(componentId, state, REVEAL_CONFIG);

  if (reveal === "absent") {
    // Render the REAL component (on its neutral token fallbacks) rather than a
    // labeled box, so the zero state reads as a genuine, if unresolved, design
    // system — the user sees an actual button/card/input taking shape. Muted
    // (desaturated + faded) to communicate "not decided yet"; it gains colour
    // and sharpness as the relevant token groups earn confidence.
    return (
      <div
        data-component={componentId}
        data-reveal="absent"
        title={`${label} — resolves as the interview learns your preferences`}
        style={{
          display: "inline-flex",
          maxWidth: "100%",
          filter: "grayscale(0.85) opacity(0.4)",
          transition: REVEAL_TRANSITION,
        }}
      >
        <Entry />
      </div>
    );
  }

Now the bento layout + zero-state educational note (items 2 & 6). Let me read the full PreviewPane:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:15:51.809Z

{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:15:51.819Z
1	/**
2	 * PreviewPane — the right pane (V0_PLAN.md "Studio shell design" layout:
3	 * paper card + docked controls bar + header with title & Export button).
4	 * Renders the manifest's component library through `renderComponent`
5	 * inside the region-select overlay, and the controls bar underneath.
6	 *
7	 * Defaults to Workstream A's real renderer (`@/preview`'s `renderComponent`,
8	 * with reveal states) rather than the Phase-0 gray-box placeholder — Phase 2
9	 * "Mount A's real preview into B's Session preview slot ... replacing the
10	 * placeholder path". Callers (tests, dev harnesses) can still override via
11	 * the `renderComponent` prop, e.g. to exercise the placeholder explicitly.
12	 *
13	 * Pending-preview values (from ControlsBar) are applied client-side only
14	 * for this render — never fed into applyPatch — and discarded whenever the
15	 * real beliefState changes. Reset happens inline during render (React's
16	 * "adjust state while rendering" pattern, not a setState-in-effect, which
17	 * the react-hooks/set-state-in-effect rule flags as cascading-render-prone)
18	 * by comparing against the last-seen beliefState reference: the moment a
19	 * new one shows up — the model's own patch has landed — `pending` is
20	 * cleared in the same render pass, before paint.
21	 */
22	"use client";
23	
24	import { useState, type CSSProperties } from "react";
25	import { COMPONENT_MANIFEST, resolveTokens, type BeliefState, type RenderComponent } from "@/contracts";
26	import { renderComponent as realRenderComponent } from "@/preview/renderComponent";
27	import { lookupTokenValue } from "../controls/tokenLookup";
28	import { withPendingPreview, type PendingPreviewEntry } from "../controls/pendingPreview";
29	import { RegionSelectOverlay } from "../region/RegionSelectOverlay";
30	import { ControlsBar } from "../controls/ControlsBar";
31	import { rationaleClaimsFor } from "./rationaleLookup";
32	
33	export interface PreviewPaneProps {
34	  beliefState: BeliefState;
35	  disabled?: boolean;
36	  onRegionComment: (target: string, text: string) => void;
37	  onControlMessage: (target: string, text: string) => void;
38	  onExport?: () => void;
39	  renderComponent?: RenderComponent;
40	  /** True when the agent has signaled confident completion this turn
41	   * (Phase 2 "Completion state") — shows a small badge next to the header
42	   * title so it's visible even if the chat-panel CTA has scrolled away. */
43	  completed?: boolean;
44	}
45	
46	export function PreviewPane({
47	  beliefState,
48	  disabled,
49	  onRegionComment,
50	  onControlMessage,
51	  onExport,
52	  renderComponent = realRenderComponent,
53	  completed,
54	}: PreviewPaneProps) {
55	  const [pending, setPending] = useState<PendingPreviewEntry[]>([]);
56	  const [lastSeenBeliefState, setLastSeenBeliefState] = useState(beliefState);
57	
58	  // Discard pending previews the moment a new (real) belief state arrives —
59	  // IMPLEMENTATION.md #4: "the canvas settles to *the model's* truth ...
60	  // discarded when the next belief state arrives". Done inline during
61	  // render (not in an effect) so there's no extra commit/flash of stale
62	  // pending values before the reset takes effect.
63	  if (beliefState !== lastSeenBeliefState) {
64	    setLastSeenBeliefState(beliefState);
65	    setPending([]);
66	  }
67	
68	  const displayState = withPendingPreview(beliefState, pending);
69	
70	  function handlePendingChange(dottedRef: string, $value: string | number, $type: "color" | "dimension") {
71	    setPending((prev) => [...prev.filter((p) => p.dottedRef !== dottedRef), { dottedRef, $value, $type }]);
72	  }
73	
74	  function resolve(dottedRef: string): string | number | undefined {
75	    return lookupTokenValue(displayState, dottedRef);
76	  }
77	
78	  return (
79	    <div className="flex h-full flex-col" data-testid="preview-pane">
80	      <header className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-app-border px-6 py-3">
81	        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
82	          <div>
83	            <p className="text-sm font-medium text-app-text">
84	              {beliefState.meta.product || "Untitled session"}
85	            </p>
86	            <p className="text-xs text-app-text-muted">design system preview</p>
87	          </div>
88	          {completed && (
89	            <span
90	              className="rounded-app-pill px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-medium text-app-positive shadow-[0_0_0_1px_var(--app-positive)]"
91	              data-testid="completion-badge"
92	            >
93	              Ready to export
94	            </span>
95	          )}
96	        </div>
97	        <button
98	          type="button"
99	          onClick={onExport}
100	          disabled={!onExport}
101	          className="rounded-app-pill bg-app-accent px-4 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium text-white transition hover:bg-app-accent-hover disabled:opacity-60 disabled:hover:bg-app-accent"
102	          data-testid="export-button"
103	        >
104	          Export
105	        </button>
106	      </header>
107	
108	      <div className="flex flex-1 items-center justify-center overflow-auto p-8">
109	        <div
110	          className="ds-preview-root h-full w-full rounded-app-lg p-6 shadow-app-paper"
111	          data-testid="ds-preview-root"
112	          style={resolveTokens(displayState) as CSSProperties}
113	        >
114	          <RegionSelectOverlay
115	            resolve={resolve}
116	            onSubmitComment={onRegionComment}
117	            disabled={disabled}
118	            rationaleFor={(componentId) => rationaleClaimsFor(beliefState, componentId)}
119	          >
120	            <div className="flex flex-wrap items-start gap-4" data-testid="component-grid">
121	              {COMPONENT_MANIFEST.map((entry) => (
122	                <div key={entry.componentId} data-testid={`component-slot-${entry.componentId}`}>
123	                  {renderComponent(displayState, entry.componentId)}
124	                </div>
125	              ))}
126	            </div>
127	          </RegionSelectOverlay>
128	        </div>
129	      </div>
130	
131	      <div className="border-t border-app-border px-6 py-3">
132	        <ControlsBar
133	          beliefState={displayState}
134	          disabled={disabled}
135	          onPendingChange={handlePendingChange}
136	          onSendMessage={onControlMessage}
137	        />
138	      </div>
139	    </div>
140	  );
141	}
142

Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:16:52.738Z

Now the bento layout + zero-state note. Let me add a bento arrangement constant and restructure the preview content:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:16:57.697Z
✏️ Edit PreviewPane.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx
−
import { rationaleClaimsFor } from "./rationaleLookup";

export interface PreviewPaneProps {
+
import { rationaleClaimsFor } from "./rationaleLookup";

/** Bento arrangement of the exemplar library on the paper canvas: a 6-column
 * grid where each component gets a cell sized to its nature (nav spans the
 * full width, card + heading are wide, the smaller controls share a row).
 * Ordered for the grid, not the manifest. */
const BENTO_LAYOUT: { id: string; span: string }[] = [
  { id: "nav.default", span: "col-span-6" },
  { id: "card.default", span: "col-span-3" },
  { id: "heading.default", span: "col-span-3" },
  { id: "button.primary", span: "col-span-2" },
  { id: "input.text", span: "col-span-2" },
  { id: "badge.default", span: "col-span-2" },
];

export interface PreviewPaneProps {
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:16:57.716Z

Assistant2026-07-03T21:17:05.439Z
✏️ Edit PreviewPane.tsx
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/preview/PreviewPane.tsx
−
  const displayState = withPendingPreview(beliefState, pending);
+
  const displayState = withPendingPreview(beliefState, pending);
  // Zero state: the agent hasn't committed any token group yet. Show a short
  // explainer so the bare canvas reads as "about to fill in", not broken.
  const isZeroState = Object.keys(beliefState.groups).length === 0;

Now item 4 — sessions switching. Let me look at the sessions index and how selection is wired:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:17:50.462Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/sessions/sessionsIndex.ts"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:17:50.469Z
1	/**
2	 * Lightweight localStorage-backed index of past session SUMMARIES (not full
3	 * belief states) so the sessions rail (SessionsRail.tsx) can list previous
4	 * sessions without loading their full state. Deliberately a separate
5	 * localStorage key from the main session snapshot
6	 * (src/shell/state/sessionStorage.ts's `mydsstudio:session:v1`) — this is
7	 * just an index of what exists, independently versioned.
8	 *
9	 * Chrome-only concern (feeds the `--app-*` rail); persistence here is
10	 * best-effort, same convention as sessionStorage.ts: guard SSR, wrap in
11	 * try/catch, never throw, discard malformed data as empty.
12	 */
13	
14	export interface SessionSummary {
15	  id: string;
16	  title: string; // product name or "Untitled session"
17	  subtitle?: string; // short status/context line, optional
18	  updatedAt: string; // ISO timestamp
19	  status?: "active" | "idle";
20	}
21	
22	export const SESSIONS_INDEX_KEY = "mydsstudio:sessions:index:v1";
23	
24	/** Cap on stored summaries — oldest entries beyond this are dropped on write. */
25	const MAX_SESSIONS = 20;
26	
27	function readAll(): SessionSummary[] {
28	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return [];
29	  try {
30	    const raw = window.localStorage.getItem(SESSIONS_INDEX_KEY);
31	    if (!raw) return [];
32	    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(raw);
33	    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return [];
34	    return parsed.filter(
35	      (entry): entry is SessionSummary =>
36	        !!entry &&
37	        typeof entry === "object" &&
38	        typeof (entry as SessionSummary).id === "string" &&
39	        typeof (entry as SessionSummary).title === "string" &&
40	        typeof (entry as SessionSummary).updatedAt === "string",
41	    );
42	  } catch {
43	    return [];
44	  }
45	}
46	
47	function writeAll(sessions: SessionSummary[]): void {
48	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
49	  try {
50	    window.localStorage.setItem(SESSIONS_INDEX_KEY, JSON.stringify(sessions));
51	  } catch {
52	    // Persistence is best-effort — never throw.
53	  }
54	}
55	
56	/** Returns stored session summaries, newest first (by `updatedAt` desc).
57	 * Returns `[]` on SSR or any read/parse failure. */
58	export function listSessions(): SessionSummary[] {
59	  const sessions = readAll();
60	  return [...sessions].sort(
61	    (a, b) => new Date(b.updatedAt).getTime() - new Date(a.updatedAt).getTime(),
62	  );
63	}
64	
65	/** Upserts a summary by id (updating `updatedAt`), capping the stored list
66	 * at the `MAX_SESSIONS` newest entries. No-op on SSR or storage failure. */
67	export function recordSession(summary: SessionSummary): void {
68	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
69	  try {
70	    const sessions = readAll();
71	    const withoutExisting = sessions.filter((s) => s.id !== summary.id);
72	    const next = [...withoutExisting, summary]
73	      .sort((a, b) => new Date(b.updatedAt).getTime() - new Date(a.updatedAt).getTime())
74	      .slice(0, MAX_SESSIONS);
75	    writeAll(next);
76	  } catch {
77	    // no-op — persistence is best-effort
78	  }
79	}
80	
81	/** Removes a session summary by id. No-op on SSR or storage failure. */
82	export function removeSession(id: string): void {
83	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
84	  try {
85	    const sessions = readAll().filter((s) => s.id !== id);
86	    writeAll(sessions);
87	  } catch {
88	    // no-op — persistence is best-effort
89	  }
90	}
91

Now sessions switching (item 4). Let me read the storage module to add per-session snapshot persistence:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:21:12.939Z
⚙ Read
{
  "file_path": "/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/state/sessionStorage.ts"
}
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:21:12.950Z
1	/**
2	 * localStorage snapshot/restore of session state (V0_PLAN.md Workstream B
3	 * "Session UI shell"). Persists the belief state + a UI-side transcript
4	 * (chat/interaction history the shell needs to redraw — not part of the
5	 * frozen BeliefState.events log, which is the model's append-only record).
6	 *
7	 * Gated on `schemaVersion`: BeliefState.schemaVersion is a literal 1
8	 * (src/contracts/beliefState.ts). A mismatched or missing version must be
9	 * discarded, not crash the app (AGENTS.md invariant list references this
10	 * requirement explicitly for Workstream B's restore path).
11	 *
12	 * Keyed per turn-mode ("live" vs "demo", see turn/useTurnMode.ts): the two
13	 * modes drive fundamentally different turn sources (RealTurnAgent's
14	 * `/api/turn` bookkeeping vs FakeTurnAgent's scripted driver), each authored
15	 * to start from an empty belief state (Session.tsx's doc comment: "there's
16	 * no coherent way to splice a mid-session belief state into either turn
17	 * source in the other's shoes"). A single shared key let a demo session's
18	 * transcript get restored into a live-mode mount (and vice versa) whenever
19	 * mode resolution changed between page loads — e.g. the live health probe
20	 * flips from unavailable to available, or a live turn fails and demotes to
21	 * demo — which produced a stuck-looking shell: the restored transcript's
22	 * `kickedOffRef` latch (useSession.ts) skips the kickoff turn entirely, so
23	 * the wrong turn source's stale history sits there with no way to progress.
24	 * Namespacing the storage key by mode makes the two sessions fully
25	 * independent, so a mode switch always resumes (or starts fresh) the
26	 * correct session.
27	 */
28	import { BeliefStateSchema, type BeliefState } from "@/contracts";
29	import type { TranscriptEntry } from "./transcript";
30	
31	const STORAGE_KEY_PREFIX = "mydsstudio:session:v1";
32	
33	/** Bump alongside BeliefState.schemaVersion if the persisted envelope shape
34	 * itself changes in an incompatible way. Independent of BeliefState's own
35	 * schemaVersion field, which is checked separately below. */
36	const ENVELOPE_VERSION = 1;
37	
38	export interface PersistedSession {
39	  envelopeVersion: number;
40	  beliefState: BeliefState;
41	  transcript: TranscriptEntry[];
42	  savedAt: string;
43	}
44	
45	/** Mirrors turn/useTurnMode.ts's TurnMode without importing it — this module
46	 * is a low-level storage primitive and shouldn't pull in the mode-resolution
47	 * hook (and its own "use client" + fetch-health-probe baggage) just for a
48	 * union of two string literals. */
49	export type SessionStorageMode = "live" | "demo";
50	
51	function storageKey(mode: SessionStorageMode): string {
52	  return `${STORAGE_KEY_PREFIX}:${mode}`;
53	}
54	
55	export function saveSession(
56	  mode: SessionStorageMode,
57	  beliefState: BeliefState,
58	  transcript: TranscriptEntry[],
59	): void {
60	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
61	  try {
62	    const payload: PersistedSession = {
63	      envelopeVersion: ENVELOPE_VERSION,
64	      beliefState,
65	      transcript,
66	      savedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
67	    };
68	    window.localStorage.setItem(storageKey(mode), JSON.stringify(payload));
69	  } catch {
70	    // Storage can fail (quota, private mode, disabled) — persistence is a
71	    // convenience, never a hard requirement for the session to function.
72	  }
73	}
74	
75	/**
76	 * Restores a session from localStorage. Returns null on: no snapshot,
77	 * malformed JSON, envelope version mismatch, or a BeliefState whose
78	 * `schemaVersion` doesn't match the current contract (z.literal(1)) —
79	 * every failure mode discards silently rather than throwing, per the
80	 * gate's "discard, not crash" requirement.
81	 */
82	export function restoreSession(mode: SessionStorageMode): PersistedSession | null {
83	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;
84	  try {
85	    const raw = window.localStorage.getItem(storageKey(mode));
86	    if (!raw) return null;
87	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<PersistedSession>;
88	    if (parsed.envelopeVersion !== ENVELOPE_VERSION) return null;
89	
90	    const beliefStateResult = BeliefStateSchema.safeParse(parsed.beliefState);
91	    if (!beliefStateResult.success) return null;
92	    // Redundant with the schema (schemaVersion is z.literal(1)) but kept
93	    // explicit since it's the exact clause the gate calls out.
94	    if (beliefStateResult.data.schemaVersion !== 1) return null;
95	
96	    const transcript = Array.isArray(parsed.transcript) ? parsed.transcript : [];
97	
98	    return {
99	      envelopeVersion: ENVELOPE_VERSION,
100	      beliefState: beliefStateResult.data,
101	      transcript: transcript as TranscriptEntry[],
102	      savedAt: typeof parsed.savedAt === "string" ? parsed.savedAt : new Date().toISOString(),
103	    };
104	  } catch {
105	    return null;
106	  }
107	}
108	
109	export function clearSession(mode: SessionStorageMode): void {
110	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
111	  try {
112	    window.localStorage.removeItem(storageKey(mode));
113	  } catch {
114	    // ignore
115	  }
116	}
117	
118	/** Exposed for tests/debugging that need the literal key a given mode
119	 * resolves to (e.g. seeding/inspecting localStorage directly). */
120	export function sessionStorageKeyFor(mode: SessionStorageMode): string {
121	  return storageKey(mode);
122	}
123
Assistant2026-07-03T21:21:53.711Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T21:21:54.146Z

I'll add per-session-id snapshots and wire selection to restore them. First the storage functions:

Assistant2026-07-03T21:22:02.296Z
✏️ Edit sessionStorage.ts
/Users/sammysam/Workspace/mydsstudio/src/shell/state/sessionStorage.ts
−
export function clearSession(mode: SessionStorageMode): void {
+
/** Per-session-id snapshot store (independent of the per-mode "current
 * session" key above), so the sessions rail can switch to a specific past
 * session by loading its full belief state + transcript. Same envelope +
 * validation as the per-mode store. */
const BY_ID_KEY_PREFIX = "mydsstudio:session:byid:v1";

export function saveSessionById(
  id: string,
  beliefState: BeliefState,
  transcript: TranscriptEntry[],
): void {
  if (typeof window === "undefined" || !id) return;
  try {
    const payload: PersistedSession = {
      envelopeVersion: ENVELOPE_VERSION,
      beliefState,
      transcript,
      savedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
    window.localStorage.setItem(`${BY_ID_KEY_PREFIX}:${id}`, JSON.stringify(payload));
  } catch {
    // best-effort, never throw
  }
}

export function restoreSessionById(id: string): PersistedSession | null {
  if (typeof window === "undefined" || !id) return null;
  try {
    const raw = window.localStorage.getItem(`${BY_ID_KEY_PREFIX}:${id}`);
    if (!raw) return null;
    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<PersistedSession>;
    if (parsed.envelopeVersion !== ENVELOPE_VERSION) return null;
    const result = BeliefStateSchema.safeParse(parsed.beliefState);
    if (!result.success || result.data.schemaVersion !== 1) return null;
    const transcript = Array.isArray(parsed.transcript) ? parsed.transcript : [];
    return {
      envelopeVersion: ENVELOPE_VERSION,
      beliefState: result.data,
      transcript: transcript as TranscriptEntry[],
      savedAt: typeof parsed.savedAt === "string" ? parsed.savedAt : new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

export function clearSession(mode: SessionStorageMode): void {
Tool reply2026-07-03T21:22:02.310Z
